content.content:
1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"encoding/json"
     6â†’	"fmt"
     7â†’	"log/slog"
     8â†’	"net/http"
     9â†’	"strings"
    10â†’	"time"
    11â†’
    12â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
    13â†’
    14â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    15â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/agent/llmagent"
    16â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
    17â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin"
    18â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin/loggingplugin"
    19â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/runner"
    20â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/session"
    21â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    22â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/agenttool"
    23â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/geminitool"
    24â†’
    25â†’	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    26â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    27â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    28â†’)
    29â†’
    30â†’// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    31â†’// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    32â†’// the resulting semantic release.
    33â†’type OrchestratorStore interface {
    34â†’	AgentDataStore
    35â†’	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    36â†’	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    37â†’	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    38â†’	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    39â†’	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    40â†’	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    41â†’	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    42â†’	ListSourcesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.Source, int, error)
    43â†’	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    44â†’}
    45â†’
    46â†’const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    47â†’
    48â†’Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    49â†’
    50â†’Use the available tools to:
    51â†’1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    52â†’2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    53â†’3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
    54â†’4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    55â†’5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
    56â†’   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).
    57â†’
    58â†’CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    59â†’Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting â€” just the raw JSON.
    60â†’
    61â†’For download_links: prefer direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) over generic release pages. Include the general release page URL as well, but prioritize direct download links when available.
    62â†’
    63â†’The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    64â†’{
    65â†’  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
    66â†’  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    67â†’  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    68â†’  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    69â†’  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    70â†’  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
    71â†’  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    72â†’  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
    73â†’  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
    74â†’  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    75â†’}`
    76â†’
    77â†’// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    78â†’// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    79â†’// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    80â†’func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    81â†’	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    82â†’	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    83â†’		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    84â†’	}
    85â†’	instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    86â†’
    87â†’	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig)
    88â†’	if err != nil {
    89â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    90â†’	}
    91â†’
    92â†’	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    93â†’	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    94â†’	if err != nil {
    95â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    96â†’	}
    97â†’
    98â†’	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
    99â†’	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   100â†’		Name:        "data_agent",
   101â†’		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   102â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   103â†’		Tools:       functionTools,
   104â†’	})
   105â†’	if err != nil {
   106â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   107â†’	}
   108â†’
   109â†’	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   110â†’	var searchTool tool.Tool
   111â†’	var searchModel model.LLM
   112â†’	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   113â†’	case "openai":
   114â†’		searchTool = oaimodel.WebSearch{}
   115â†’		// OpenAI web search requires a search-capable model.
   116â†’		searchModelName := llmConfig.OpenAISearchModel
   117â†’		if searchModelName == "" {
   118â†’			searchModelName = "gpt-5-search-api"
   119â†’		}
   120â†’		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, LLMConfig{
   121â†’			Provider:      "openai",
   122â†’			Model:         searchModelName,
   123â†’			OpenAIAPIKey:  llmConfig.OpenAIAPIKey,
   124â†’			OpenAIBaseURL: llmConfig.OpenAIBaseURL,
   125â†’		})
   126â†’		if err != nil {
   127â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   128â†’		}
   129â†’	default: // gemini
   130â†’		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   131â†’		searchModel = llmModel
   132â†’	}
   133â†’
   134â†’	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   135â†’		Name:        "search_agent",
   136â†’		Description: "Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues.",
   137â†’		Model:       searchModel,
   138â†’		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   139â†’	})
   140â†’	if err != nil {
   141â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   142â†’	}
   143â†’
   144â†’	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   145â†’	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   146â†’		Name:        "release_analyst",
   147â†’		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   148â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   149â†’		Instruction: instruction,
   150â†’		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   151â†’			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   152â†’			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   153â†’		},
   154â†’	})
   155â†’}
   156â†’
   157â†’// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   158â†’// release matching the target version.
   159â†’func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   160â†’	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   161â†’	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   162â†’	if err != nil {
   163â†’		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   164â†’	}
   165â†’	if len(sources) == 0 {
   166â†’		return true, nil
   167â†’	}
   168â†’
   169â†’	for _, src := range sources {
   170â†’		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   171â†’		if err != nil {
   172â†’			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   173â†’		}
   174â†’		if !has {
   175â†’			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   176â†’				"project_id", projectID,
   177â†’				"source_id", src.ID,
   178â†’				"version", version,
   179â†’			)
   180â†’			return false, nil
   181â†’		}
   182â†’	}
   183â†’	return true, nil
   184â†’}
   185â†’
   186â†’// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   187â†’// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   188â†’// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   189â†’type Orchestrator struct {
   190â†’	store     OrchestratorStore
   191â†’	llmConfig LLMConfig
   192â†’	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   193â†’}
   194â†’
   195â†’// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   196â†’// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   197â†’// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   198â†’func NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   199â†’	switch cfg.Provider {
   200â†’	case "openai":
   201â†’		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   202â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   203â†’		}
   204â†’	default: // gemini
   205â†’		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   206â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   207â†’		}
   208â†’	}
   209â†’	return &Orchestrator{
   210â†’		store:     store,
   211â†’		llmConfig: cfg,
   212â†’		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   213â†’	}, nil
   214â†’}
   215â†’
   216â†’// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   217â†’//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   218â†’//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   219â†’//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   220â†’//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   221â†’//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   222â†’//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   223â†’//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   224â†’func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   225â†’	// Mark as running.
   226â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   227â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   228â†’	}
   229â†’
   230â†’	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   231â†’	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   232â†’	if err != nil {
   233â†’		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   234â†’		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   235â†’		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   236â†’			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   237â†’				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   238â†’		}
   239â†’		return err
   240â†’	}
   241â†’
   242â†’	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   243â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   244â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   245â†’	}
   246â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   247â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   248â†’	}
   249â†’
   250â†’	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   251â†’	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   252â†’
   253â†’	return nil
   254â†’}
   255â†’
   256â†’// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   257â†’// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   258â†’type agentResult struct {
   259â†’	semanticReleaseID string
   260â†’	version           string
   261â†’	reportText        string
   262â†’	projectName       string
   263â†’}
   264â†’
   265â†’// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   266â†’// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   267â†’func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   268â†’	// Load project.
   269â†’	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   270â†’	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   271â†’	if err != nil {
   272â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   273â†’	}
   274â†’	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   275â†’
   276â†’	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   277â†’	version := run.Version
   278â†’	if version == "" {
   279â†’		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   280â†’		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   281â†’			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   282â†’		}
   283â†’	}
   284â†’
   285â†’	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   286â†’	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   287â†’		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   288â†’		"version", version)
   289â†’	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   290â†’	if err != nil {
   291â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   292â†’	}
   293â†’
   294â†’	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   295â†’	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   296â†’	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   297â†’		AppName: "changelogue",
   298â†’		UserID:  "system",
   299â†’	})
   300â†’	if err != nil {
   301â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   302â†’	}
   303â†’	sess := createResp.Session
   304â†’
   305â†’	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   306â†’	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   307â†’		AppName:        "changelogue",
   308â†’		Agent:          agentInstance,
   309â†’		SessionService: sessionService,
   310â†’		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   311â†’			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   312â†’			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   313â†’		},
   314â†’	})
   315â†’	if err != nil {
   316â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   317â†’	}
   318â†’
   319â†’	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   320â†’	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   321â†’	var userPrompt string
   322â†’	if version != "" {
   323â†’		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   324â†’	} else {
   325â†’		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   326â†’	}
   327â†’	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   328â†’
   329â†’	var finalText string
   330â†’	eventCount := 0
   331â†’	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   332â†’		if err != nil {
   333â†’			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   334â†’				"run_id", run.ID,
   335â†’				"event_count", eventCount,
   336â†’				"err", err,
   337â†’			)
   338â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   339â†’		}
   340â†’		eventCount++
   341â†’		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   342â†’			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   343â†’				if part.Text != "" {
   344â†’					finalText += part.Text
   345â†’				}
   346â†’			}
   347â†’		}
   348â†’	}
   349â†’	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   350â†’		"run_id", run.ID,
   351â†’		"event_count", eventCount,
   352â†’		"output_length", len(finalText),
   353â†’	)
   354â†’
   355â†’	if finalText == "" {
   356â†’		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   357â†’			"run_id", run.ID,
   358â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   359â†’			"event_count", eventCount,
   360â†’		)
   361â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   362â†’	}
   363â†’
   364â†’	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   365â†’	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   366â†’	if err != nil {
   367â†’		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   368â†’			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   369â†’		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   370â†’		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   371â†’			Summary: finalText,
   372â†’		}
   373â†’	}
   374â†’
   375â†’	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   376â†’	if err != nil {
   377â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   378â†’	}
   379â†’
   380â†’	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   381â†’	// Only link releases that match the target version.
   382â†’	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 200, false)
   383â†’	if err != nil {
   384â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   385â†’	}
   386â†’	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   387â†’	for _, r := range releases {
   388â†’		if r.Version == version {
   389â†’			releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   390â†’		}
   391â†’	}
   392â†’
   393â†’	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   394â†’	srVersion := version
   395â†’	if srVersion == "" {
   396â†’		srVersion = "unknown"
   397â†’		if len(releases) > 0 {
   398â†’			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   399â†’		}
   400â†’	}
   401â†’
   402â†’	now := time.Now()
   403â†’	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   404â†’		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   405â†’		Version:     srVersion,
   406â†’		Report:      reportJSON,
   407â†’		Status:      "completed",
   408â†’		CompletedAt: &now,
   409â†’	}
   410â†’
   411â†’	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   412â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   413â†’	}
   414â†’
   415â†’	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   416â†’		"run_id", run.ID,
   417â†’		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   418â†’		"version", sr.Version,
   419â†’	)
   420â†’
   421â†’	return &agentResult{
   422â†’		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   423â†’		version:           srVersion,
   424â†’		reportText:        finalText,
   425â†’		projectName:       project.Name,
   426â†’	}, nil
   427â†’}
   428â†’
   429â†’// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   430â†’// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   431â†’func defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   432â†’	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   433â†’	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   434â†’		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   435â†’		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   436â†’		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   437â†’	}
   438â†’}
   439â†’
   440â†’// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   441â†’// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   442â†’// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   443â†’func (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   444â†’	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   445â†’	if err != nil {
   446â†’		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   447â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   448â†’		return
   449â†’	}
   450â†’	if len(subs) == 0 {
   451â†’		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   452â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   453â†’		return
   454â†’	}
   455â†’
   456â†’	senders := defaultSenders()
   457â†’
   458â†’	msg := routing.Notification{
   459â†’		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   460â†’		Body:        result.reportText,
   461â†’		Version:     result.version,
   462â†’		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   463â†’	}
   464â†’
   465â†’	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   466â†’	if o.publicURL != "" {
   467â†’		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   468â†’	}
   469â†’
   470â†’	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   471â†’	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   472â†’	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   473â†’		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   474â†’		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   475â†’		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   476â†’	}
   477â†’
   478â†’	for _, sub := range subs {
   479â†’		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   480â†’		if err != nil {
   481â†’			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   482â†’				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   483â†’			continue
   484â†’		}
   485â†’
   486â†’		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   487â†’		if !ok {
   488â†’			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   489â†’			continue
   490â†’		}
   491â†’
   492â†’		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   493â†’			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   494â†’				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   495â†’		} else {
   496â†’			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   497â†’				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   498â†’		}
   499â†’	}
   500â†’}
   501â†’
   502â†’// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   503â†’// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   504â†’func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   505â†’	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   506â†’	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   507â†’	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   508â†’		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   509â†’		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   510â†’			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   511â†’		}
   512â†’		// Remove closing fence.
   513â†’		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   514â†’			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   515â†’		}
   516â†’		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   517â†’	}
   518â†’
   519â†’	var report models.SemanticReport
   520â†’	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   521â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   522â†’	}
   523â†’
   524â†’	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   525â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   526â†’	}
   527â†’
   528â†’	return &report, nil
   529â†’}
   530â†’

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"fmt"
     6â†’
     7â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     8â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model/gemini"
     9â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
    10â†’
    11â†’	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    12â†’)
    13â†’
    14â†’// LLMConfig holds the configuration for creating an LLM model.
    15â†’type LLMConfig struct {
    16â†’	Provider string // "gemini" or "openai"
    17â†’	Model    string // e.g. "gemini-2.5-flash", "gpt-5.2"
    18â†’
    19â†’	// Gemini
    20â†’	GoogleAPIKey string
    21â†’
    22â†’	// OpenAI
    23â†’	OpenAIAPIKey      string
    24â†’	OpenAIBaseURL     string // defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1
    25â†’	OpenAISearchModel string // search-capable model for web search sub-agent
    26â†’}
    27â†’
    28â†’// NewLLMModel creates a model.LLM based on the configured provider.
    29â†’func NewLLMModel(ctx context.Context, cfg LLMConfig) (model.LLM, error) {
    30â†’	switch cfg.Provider {
    31â†’	case "gemini", "":
    32â†’		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
    33â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is required for gemini provider")
    34â†’		}
    35â†’		return gemini.NewModel(ctx, cfg.Model, &genai.ClientConfig{
    36â†’			APIKey: cfg.GoogleAPIKey,
    37â†’		})
    38â†’
    39â†’	case "openai":
    40â†’		return oaimodel.NewModel(ctx, cfg.Model, oaimodel.Config{
    41â†’			APIKey:  cfg.OpenAIAPIKey,
    42â†’			BaseURL: cfg.OpenAIBaseURL,
    43â†’		})
    44â†’
    45â†’	default:
    46â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown LLM provider: %q (supported: gemini, openai)", cfg.Provider)
    47â†’	}
    48â†’}
    49â†’

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"encoding/json"
     6â†’	"fmt"
     7â†’	"log/slog"
     8â†’	"time"
     9â†’
    10â†’	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    11â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    12â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    13â†’)
    14â†’
    15â†’// AgentWorker is a River worker that processes AgentJobArgs by running the
    16â†’// LLM agent orchestrator. When a job is dequeued, it loads the corresponding
    17â†’// agent run from the store and delegates to Orchestrator.RunAgent.
    18â†’type AgentWorker struct {
    19â†’	river.WorkerDefaults[queue.AgentJobArgs]
    20â†’	orchestrator *Orchestrator
    21â†’	store        OrchestratorStore
    22â†’}
    23â†’
    24â†’// NewAgentWorker creates a new AgentWorker backed by the given orchestrator
    25â†’// and store.
    26â†’func NewAgentWorker(orchestrator *Orchestrator, store OrchestratorStore) *AgentWorker {
    27â†’	return &AgentWorker{
    28â†’		orchestrator: orchestrator,
    29â†’		store:        store,
    30â†’	}
    31â†’}
    32â†’
    33â†’// Timeout overrides River's default 60-second job timeout. Agent runs involve
    34â†’// multiple LLM round-trips (root agent + sub-agents), each of which can take
    35â†’// 10-30 seconds, so we allow 5 minutes total.
    36â†’func (w *AgentWorker) Timeout(_ *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) time.Duration {
    37â†’	return 5 * time.Minute
    38â†’}
    39â†’
    40â†’// Work processes a single AgentJobArgs job. It loads the agent run from the
    41â†’// database and runs the LLM agent through the orchestrator. If the project
    42â†’// has WaitForAllSources enabled and not all sources have the target version,
    43â†’// the job is snoozed for 5 minutes via river.JobSnooze.
    44â†’func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {
    45â†’	slog.Info("agent worker picked up job",
    46â†’		"job_id", job.ID,
    47â†’		"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    48â†’		"attempt", job.Attempt,
    49â†’	)
    50â†’
    51â†’	run, err := w.store.GetAgentRun(ctx, job.Args.AgentRunID)
    52â†’	if err != nil {
    53â†’		slog.Error("agent worker failed to load agent run",
    54â†’			"job_id", job.ID,
    55â†’			"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    56â†’			"err", err,
    57â†’		)
    58â†’		return fmt.Errorf("get agent run: %w", err)
    59â†’	}
    60â†’
    61â†’	// Check multi-source waiting: if the run has a version and the project
    62â†’	// has WaitForAllSources enabled, verify all sources have reported a
    63â†’	// release for the target version before running the agent.
    64â†’	if run.Version != "" {
    65â†’		project, err := w.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
    66â†’		if err != nil {
    67â†’			slog.Error("agent worker failed to load project",
    68â†’				"job_id", job.ID,
    69â†’				"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    70â†’				"err", err,
    71â†’			)
    72â†’			return fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
    73â†’		}
    74â†’
    75â†’		var rules models.AgentRules
    76â†’		if len(project.AgentRules) > 0 {
    77â†’			if err := json.Unmarshal(project.AgentRules, &rules); err != nil {
    78â†’				slog.Warn("agent worker: failed to unmarshal agent rules, proceeding without wait",
    79â†’					"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
    80â†’			}
    81â†’		}
    82â†’
    83â†’		if rules.WaitForAllSources {
    84â†’			ready, err := w.orchestrator.checkAllSourcesReady(ctx, run.ProjectID, run.Version)
    85â†’			if err != nil {
    86â†’				slog.Error("agent worker: error checking source readiness",
    87â†’					"job_id", job.ID,
    88â†’					"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    89â†’					"version", run.Version,
    90â†’					"err", err,
    91â†’				)
    92â†’				return fmt.Errorf("check all sources ready: %w", err)
    93â†’			}
    94â†’			if !ready {
    95â†’				// After maxWaitAttempts (12 Ă— 5min = ~1 hour), proceed
    96â†’				// with partial data rather than waiting forever.
    97â†’				const maxWaitAttempts = 12
    98â†’				if job.Attempt >= maxWaitAttempts {
    99â†’					slog.Warn("agent worker: max wait attempts reached, proceeding with partial data",
   100â†’						"job_id", job.ID,
   101â†’						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   102â†’						"version", run.Version,
   103â†’						"attempts", job.Attempt,
   104â†’					)
   105â†’				} else {
   106â†’					slog.Info("agent worker: not all sources ready, snoozing",
   107â†’						"job_id", job.ID,
   108â†’						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   109â†’						"version", run.Version,
   110â†’						"attempt", job.Attempt,
   111â†’					)
   112â†’					if err := w.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "waiting"); err != nil {
   113â†’						slog.Error("failed to set waiting status", "err", err)
   114â†’					}
   115â†’					return river.JobSnooze(5 * time.Minute)
   116â†’				}
   117â†’			}
   118â†’		}
   119â†’	}
   120â†’
   121â†’	slog.Info("agent worker starting run",
   122â†’		"job_id", job.ID,
   123â†’		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   124â†’		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   125â†’		"trigger", run.Trigger,
   126â†’	)
   127â†’
   128â†’	if err := w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run); err != nil {
   129â†’		slog.Error("agent worker run failed",
   130â†’			"job_id", job.ID,
   131â†’			"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   132â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   133â†’			"err", err,
   134â†’		)
   135â†’		return err
   136â†’	}
   137â†’
   138â†’	slog.Info("agent worker run completed",
   139â†’		"job_id", job.ID,
   140â†’		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   141â†’		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   142â†’	)
   143â†’	return nil
   144â†’}
   145â†’

1â†’package openai
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"fmt"
     5â†’
     6â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
     7â†’
     8â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     9â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    10â†’)
    11â†’
    12â†’// WebSearch is a tool that enables OpenAI's built-in web search via
    13â†’// web_search_options. It mirrors geminitool.GoogleSearch by adding a
    14â†’// GoogleSearch marker to the request config, which the OpenAI wrapper
    15â†’// translates into the web_search_options HTTP body field.
    16â†’type WebSearch struct{}
    17â†’
    18â†’// Name implements tool.Tool.
    19â†’func (WebSearch) Name() string { return "web_search" }
    20â†’
    21â†’// Description implements tool.Tool.
    22â†’func (WebSearch) Description() string {
    23â†’	return "Search the web for additional context about a release."
    24â†’}
    25â†’
    26â†’// IsLongRunning implements tool.Tool.
    27â†’func (WebSearch) IsLongRunning() bool { return false }
    28â†’
    29â†’// ProcessRequest implements toolinternal.RequestProcessor by adding a
    30â†’// GoogleSearch marker to the LLM request. The OpenAI model wrapper
    31â†’// detects this marker and emits web_search_options in the HTTP body.
    32â†’func (WebSearch) ProcessRequest(_ tool.Context, req *model.LLMRequest) error {
    33â†’	if req == nil {
    34â†’		return fmt.Errorf("llm request is nil")
    35â†’	}
    36â†’	if req.Config == nil {
    37â†’		req.Config = &genai.GenerateContentConfig{}
    38â†’	}
    39â†’	req.Config.Tools = append(req.Config.Tools, &genai.Tool{
    40â†’		GoogleSearch: &genai.GoogleSearch{},
    41â†’	})
    42â†’	return nil
    43â†’}
    44â†’

1â†’package main
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"flag"
     6â†’	"fmt"
     7â†’	"log"
     8â†’	"log/slog"
     9â†’	"os"
    10â†’
    11â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    12â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/cmd/launcher"
    13â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/cmd/launcher/full"
    14â†’
    15â†’	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    16â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    17â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    18â†’)
    19â†’
    20â†’func main() {
    21â†’	projectID := flag.String("project-id", "", "Project ID to scope the agent to (required)")
    22â†’	flag.Parse()
    23â†’
    24â†’	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    25â†’	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    26â†’	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    27â†’	case "debug":
    28â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    29â†’	case "warn":
    30â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    31â†’	case "error":
    32â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    33â†’	}
    34â†’	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    35â†’
    36â†’	if *projectID == "" {
    37â†’		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error: --project-id is required")
    38â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "usage: go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> [web api webui]\n")
    39â†’		os.Exit(1)
    40â†’	}
    41â†’
    42â†’	ctx := context.Background()
    43â†’
    44â†’	// Database
    45â†’	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    46â†’	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    47â†’	if err != nil {
    48â†’		log.Fatalf("database connection failed: %v", err)
    49â†’	}
    50â†’	defer pool.Close()
    51â†’
    52â†’	store := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    53â†’
    54â†’	// Validate project exists
    55â†’	project, err := store.GetProject(ctx, *projectID)
    56â†’	if err != nil {
    57â†’		log.Fatalf("project %q not found: %v", *projectID, err)
    58â†’	}
    59â†’	slog.Info("loaded project", "id", project.ID, "name", project.Name)
    60â†’
    61â†’	// LLM config (same env vars as server)
    62â†’	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    63â†’	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    64â†’	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    65â†’		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    66â†’	}
    67â†’	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    68â†’		Provider:          llmProvider,
    69â†’		Model:             envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    70â†’		GoogleAPIKey:      os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    71â†’		OpenAIAPIKey:      os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    72â†’		OpenAIBaseURL:     os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    73â†’		OpenAISearchModel: envOr("OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL", "gpt-5-search-api"),
    74â†’	}
    75â†’
    76â†’	agentInstance, err := agentpkg.BuildAgent(ctx, store, project, llmConfig, "")
    77â†’	if err != nil {
    78â†’		log.Fatalf("build agent: %v", err)
    79â†’	}
    80â†’
    81â†’	// Launch with ADK-Web UI
    82â†’	config := &launcher.Config{
    83â†’		AgentLoader: agent.NewSingleLoader(agentInstance),
    84â†’	}
    85â†’
    86â†’	l := full.NewLauncher()
    87â†’	if err := l.Execute(ctx, config, flag.Args()); err != nil {
    88â†’		log.Fatalf("launcher failed: %v\n\n%s", err, l.CommandLineSyntax())
    89â†’	}
    90â†’}
    91â†’
    92â†’func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
    93â†’	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
    94â†’		return v
    95â†’	}
    96â†’	return fallback
    97â†’}
    98â†’

1â†’package main
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"log/slog"
     6â†’	"net/http"
     7â†’	"os"
     8â†’	"os/signal"
     9â†’	"time"
    10â†’
    11â†’	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12â†’	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    17â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    18â†’)
    19â†’
    20â†’func main() {
    21â†’	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    22â†’	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    23â†’	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    24â†’	case "debug":
    25â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    26â†’	case "warn":
    27â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    28â†’	case "error":
    29â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    30â†’	}
    31â†’	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    32â†’
    33â†’	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    34â†’	defer stop()
    35â†’
    36â†’	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    37â†’	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    38â†’	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    39â†’
    40â†’	// Database
    41â†’	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    42â†’	if err != nil {
    43â†’		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    44â†’		os.Exit(1)
    45â†’	}
    46â†’	defer pool.Close()
    47â†’
    48â†’	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    49â†’		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    50â†’		os.Exit(1)
    51â†’	}
    52â†’
    53â†’	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    54â†’	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    55â†’	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    56â†’	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Register River workers
    59â†’	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    60â†’	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    61â†’	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    62â†’
    63â†’	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    64â†’	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    65â†’	// is restarted.
    66â†’	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    67â†’	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    68â†’	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    69â†’		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    70â†’	}
    71â†’	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    72â†’		Provider:          llmProvider,
    73â†’		Model:             envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    74â†’		GoogleAPIKey:      os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    75â†’		OpenAIAPIKey:      os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    76â†’		OpenAIBaseURL:     os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    77â†’		OpenAISearchModel: envOr("OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL", "gpt-5-search-api"),
    78â†’	}
    79â†’	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    80â†’	if err != nil {
    81â†’		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available â€” agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    82â†’	} else {
    83â†’		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    84â†’		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    85â†’		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    86â†’	}
    87â†’
    88â†’	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
    89â†’	if err != nil {
    90â†’		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
    91â†’		os.Exit(1)
    92â†’	}
    93â†’
    94â†’	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
    95â†’	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
    96â†’	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
    97â†’
    98â†’	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
    99â†’		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
   100â†’		os.Exit(1)
   101â†’	}
   102â†’
   103â†’	// Ingestion layer
   104â†’	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   105â†’	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   106â†’
   107â†’	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   108â†’	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   109â†’
   110â†’	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   111â†’
   112â†’	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   113â†’
   114â†’	// Serve frontend static export if available
   115â†’	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   116â†’		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   117â†’		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   118â†’	}
   119â†’
   120â†’	// Register all API v1 routes
   121â†’	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   122â†’		DB:                    pool,
   123â†’		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   124â†’		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   125â†’		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   126â†’		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   127â†’		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   128â†’		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   129â†’		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   130â†’		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   131â†’		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   132â†’		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   133â†’		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   134â†’		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   135â†’		IngestionService:      svc,
   136â†’		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   137â†’	})
   138â†’
   139â†’	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   140â†’
   141â†’	// Start polling in background
   142â†’	go orch.Run(ctx)
   143â†’
   144â†’	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY â†’ SSE bridge
   145â†’	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   146â†’
   147â†’	// Start HTTP server
   148â†’	go func() {
   149â†’		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   150â†’		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   151â†’			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   152â†’		}
   153â†’	}()
   154â†’
   155â†’	<-ctx.Done()
   156â†’	slog.Info("shutting down")
   157â†’
   158â†’	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   159â†’	defer cancel()
   160â†’	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   161â†’	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   162â†’}
   163â†’
   164â†’func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   165â†’	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   166â†’		return v
   167â†’	}
   168â†’	return fallback
   169â†’}
   170â†’

     3â†’import (

     8â†’type AgentRules struct {

    10â†’	OnMinorRelease    bool   `json:"on_minor_release,omitempty"`

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Agent Setup & LLM Analysis Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Google ADK-Go agent implementation in Changelogue. Here are the detailed findings:

---

### 1. **Agent Architecture Overview**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/ARCH.md` (lines 165-188)

The agent layer is implemented using **Google ADK-Go v0.5.0** with multi-agent orchestration:

- **Root Agent** (`release_analyst`): Orchestrates the full analysis workflow
- **Data Sub-Agent**: Queries releases and context sources from the database
- **Search Sub-Agent**: Performs web searches for additional context (provider-specific)

---

### 2. **Agent Tool Registration & Definition**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/tools.go`

Three ADK function tools are registered via `functiontool.New()`:

| Tool | Input | Output | Purpose |
|------|-------|--------|---------|
| `get_releases` | `{page, per_page}` | Paginated release list | Fetch recent releases for the project |
| `get_release_detail` | `{release_id}` | Full release with raw data | Inspect changelogs and metadata |
| `list_context_sources` | `{page, per_page}` | Context source list | Discover runbooks, docs, dashboards |

**Key code**:
```go
// tools.go: NewTools creates ADK function tools scoped to a project
func NewTools(store AgentDataStore, projectID string) ([]tool.Tool, error) {
    f := &toolFactory{store: store, projectID: projectID}
    
    getReleases, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
        Name:        "get_releases",
        Description: "Fetch recent releases for the current project...",
    }, f.getReleases)
    // ... similar for get_release_detail, list_context_sources
}
```

Tools are **project-scoped** via `toolFactory`, ensuring all queries are filtered to the specific project ID.

---

### 3. **LLM Model & Provider Support**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/model.go`

The orchestrator supports **two LLM providers**:

```go
type LLMConfig struct {
    Provider string // "gemini" or "openai"
    Model    string // e.g. "gemini-2.5-flash", "gpt-5.2"
    
    // Gemini
    GoogleAPIKey string
    
    // OpenAI
    OpenAIAPIKey      string
    OpenAIBaseURL     string
    OpenAISearchModel string // search-capable model for web search
}
```

**Provider selection logic**:
- **Gemini** (default): Uses Google's native SDK (`google.golang.org/genai`) with ADK's `gemini.NewModel()` and `geminitool.GoogleSearch`
- **OpenAI**: Uses custom adapter (`internal/agent/openai/`) implementing `model.LLM` interface with Web Search support

---

### 4. **OpenAI Adapter Implementation**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/openai.go`

The adapter provides **ADK-compatible OpenAI integration**:

- **`openaiModel` struct**: Implements `model.LLM` interface
- **`GenerateContent()`**: Converts ADK `LLMRequest` â†’ OpenAI chat completion â†’ ADK `LLMResponse`
- **Streaming**: Not implemented (always yields single response)
- **Tool conversion**: Transforms ADK `genai.Tool` definitions into OpenAI function schema
- **Web Search**: Detects `GoogleSearch` marker in request and emits `web_search_options` in HTTP body

**Example request conversion**:
```go
// OpenAI endpoint: POST /v1/chat/completions
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "messages": [...],  // ADK genai.Content â†’ OpenAI chat messages
  "tools": [...],     // ADK function declarations â†’ OpenAI function schema
  "web_search_options": {}  // enabled if search_agent is used
}
```

---

### 5. **Web Search Tool Setup**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`

The `WebSearch` tool is provider-specific:

```go
// OpenAI: custom WebSearch tool
searchTool = oaimodel.WebSearch{}

// Gemini: native GoogleSearch tool
searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
```

Both tools are registered with the search sub-agent:
```go
searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
    Name:        "search_agent",
    Description: "Search the web for additional context...",
    Model:       searchModel,
    Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},  // provider-specific
})
```

---

### 6. **Agent Orchestrator Lifecycle**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go`

The `Orchestrator` manages the complete agent run:

```go
type Orchestrator struct {
    store     OrchestratorStore
    llmConfig LLMConfig
    publicURL string
}
```

**`RunAgent()` workflow** (lines 224-254):
1. Mark run as "running"
2. Execute agent via `executeAgent()` (detailed below)
3. Parse JSON report from LLM output
4. Create `SemanticRelease` with linked `semantic_release_sources`
5. Send project-level notifications
6. Mark run as "completed"

**`executeAgent()` execution** (lines 267-427):
1. Load project config (custom `agent_prompt`)
2. Build dynamic instruction with version substitution (line 85)
3. Create LLM model via `NewLLMModel()` (supports Gemini or OpenAI)
4. Create project-scoped tools via `NewTools(store, projectID)`
5. Build multi-agent hierarchy:
   - Data agent (function tools)
   - Search agent (web search tool)
   - Root agent (agentools wrapping both)
6. Run via ADK `runner.Runner` with in-memory session
7. Capture final text response
8. Parse as JSON `SemanticReport`
9. Fallback: store raw text if parsing fails

---

### 7. **Agent Worker & River Job Integration**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/worker.go`

The `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`:

```go
func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {
    // 1. Load agent run
    run, err := w.store.GetAgentRun(ctx, job.Args.AgentRunID)
    
    // 2. Check multi-source waiting (if WaitForAllSources rule enabled)
    if run.Version != "" && rules.WaitForAllSources {
        ready, _ := w.orchestrator.checkAllSourcesReady(ctx, run.ProjectID, run.Version)
        if !ready && job.Attempt < 12 {
            return river.JobSnooze(5 * time.Minute)  // Retry in 5 min
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Execute agent
    return w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run)
}
```

**Timeout**: 5 minutes per job (accounts for multiple LLM round-trips)

---

### 8. **Job Queuing & Triggering**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/queue/jobs.go`

```go
type AgentJobArgs struct {
    AgentRunID string `json:"agent_run_id"`
    ProjectID  string `json:"project_id"`
    Version    string `json:"version"`
}

func (AgentJobArgs) Kind() string { return "agent_run" }
```

**Two triggering paths**:

1. **API Manual Trigger** (`/api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run`)
   - Files: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/agent.go` (lines 34-57)
   - Calls `store.TriggerAgentRun(projectID, trigger, version)`

2. **Auto-trigger on Release** (via NotifyWorker)
   - File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/routing/worker.go` (lines 80-150)
   - Checks project's `AgentRules` (on_major_release, on_security_patch, version_pattern, wait_for_all_sources)
   - Calls `store.EnqueueAgentRun()` if criteria match

**Transactional Outbox** (file: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/pgstore.go`, lines 892-914):
```go
func (s *PgStore) EnqueueAgentRun(ctx context.Context, projectID, trigger, version string) error {
    tx, _ := s.pool.Begin(ctx)
    // 1. INSERT agent_runs row
    tx.QueryRow(...INSERT INTO agent_runs...)
    // 2. INSERT River job in same transaction
    s.river.InsertTx(ctx, tx, queue.AgentJobArgs{...}, nil)
    // 3. Commit (zero-loss guarantee)
}
```

---

### 9. **LLM Configuration & Initialization**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/cmd/server/main.go` (lines 66-86)

Agent worker registration in main:
```go
llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    Provider:          llmProvider,
    Model:             envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    GoogleAPIKey:      os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    OpenAIAPIKey:      os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    OpenAIBaseURL:     os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    OpenAISearchModel: envOr("OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL", "gpt-5-search-api"),
}

agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
if err != nil {
    slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available â€” agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
} else {
    agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
}
```

**Deployment model**: Single binary with conditional agent worker registration based on API key availability.

---

### 10. **Agent Development CLI**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/cmd/agent/main.go`

A standalone CLI tool for interactive agent testing with ADK-Web UI:

```bash
go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> [web]
```

Uses `launcher.NewLauncher()` to start an interactive web UI for agent debugging and testing.

---

### 11. **Default Agent Instruction & Output Schema**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go` (lines 46-75)

The agent is instructed to output a **JSON `SemanticReport`** with these fields:

```json
{
  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level...",
  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes...",
  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation...",
  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation...",
  "download_commands": ["docker pull ..."],
  "download_links": ["https://..."]
}
```

The instruction includes version substitution: `{{VERSION}}` â†’ actual target version.

---

### 12. **Semantic Release Model & Persistence**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/models/semantic_release.go`

```go
type SemanticReport struct {
    Subject          string   `json:"subject"`
    Urgency          string   `json:"urgency"`
    UrgencyReason    string   `json:"urgency_reason,omitempty"`
    StatusChecks     []string `json:"status_checks"`
    ChangelogSummary string   `json:"changelog_summary"`
    // ... plus legacy fields for backward compat
}

type SemanticRelease struct {
    ID          string          `json:"id"`
    ProjectID   string          `json:"project_id"`
    Version     string          `json:"version"`
    Report      json.RawMessage `json:"report,omitempty"`  // SemanticReport as JSONB
    Status      string          `json:"status"`  // pending, processing, completed, failed
    CompletedAt *time.Time      `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
}
```

**Persistence** (orchestrator.go, lines 379-413):
- Gathers all releases matching target version
- Creates `semantic_release_sources` join table entries
- Stores full report as JSONB
- Links to agent run via `UpdateAgentRunResult()`

---

### 13. **Key Data Interfaces & Store Contracts**

**Agent Data Access** (`tools.go`, line 15):
```go
type AgentDataStore interface {
    ListReleasesByProject(ctx, projectID, page, perPage, includeExcluded)
    GetRelease(ctx, id)
    ListContextSources(ctx, projectID, page, perPage)
}
```

**Orchestrator Store** (`orchestrator.go`, lines 33-44):
```go
type OrchestratorStore interface {
    AgentDataStore
    GetProject(ctx, id)
    GetAgentRun(ctx, id)
    UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, id, status)
    CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs)
    UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, id, semanticReleaseID)
    ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, projectID)
    GetChannel(ctx, id)
    ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, page, perPage)
    HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, sourceID, version)
}
```

All implemented by `api.PgStore`.

---

### 14. **Graceful Degradation**

The system gracefully degrades when LLM API keys are unavailable:

1. **Server startup** (main.go, line 79-86):
   - `NewOrchestrator()` returns error if API key missing
   - Agent worker is NOT registered
   - Rest of system (ingestion, notifications) continues

2. **At runtime**:
   - Agent jobs remain in River queue unprocessed
   - Restart server with API key configured to resume

3. **Agent run failure**:
   - Marked as "failed" with error captured
   - JSON parse failure: falls back to storing raw text as summary

---

### Summary of Key Files

| File Path | Purpose | Lines |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/tools.go` | ADK function tool definitions (get_releases, get_release_detail, list_context_sources) | 94-220 |
| `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go` | Agent lifecycle orchestration, multi-agent hierarchy, semantic release creation | 46-529 |

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Agent Setup & LLM Analysis Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Google ADK-Go agent implementation in Changelogue. Here are the detailed findings:

---

### 1. **Agent Architecture Overview**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/ARCH.md` (lines 165-188)

The agent layer is implemented using **Google ADK-Go v0.5.0** with multi-agent orchestration:

- **Root Agent** (`release_analyst`): Orchestrates the full analysis workflow
- **Data Sub-Agent**: Queries releases and context sources from the database
- **Search Sub-Agent**: Performs web searches for additional context (provider-specific)

---

### 2. **Agent Tool Registration & Definition**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/tools.go`

Three ADK function tools are registered via `functiontool.New()`:

| Tool | Input | Output | Purpose |
|------|-------|--------|---------|
| `get_releases` | `{page, per_page}` | Paginated release list | Fetch recent releases for the project |
| `get_release_detail` | `{release_id}` | Full release with raw data | Inspect changelogs and metadata |
| `list_context_sources` | `{page, per_page}` | Context source list | Discover runbooks, docs, dashboards |

**Key code**:
```go
// tools.go: NewTools creates ADK function tools scoped to a project
func NewTools(store AgentDataStore, projectID string) ([]tool.Tool, error) {
    f := &toolFactory{store: store, projectID: projectID}
    
    getReleases, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
        Name:        "get_releases",
        Description: "Fetch recent releases for the current project...",
    }, f.getReleases)
    // ... similar for get_release_detail, list_context_sources
}
```

Tools are **project-scoped** via `toolFactory`, ensuring all queries are filtered to the specific project ID.

---

### 3. **LLM Model & Provider Support**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/model.go`

The orchestrator supports **two LLM providers**:

```go
type LLMConfig struct {
    Provider string // "gemini" or "openai"
    Model    string // e.g. "gemini-2.5-flash", "gpt-5.2"
    
    // Gemini
    GoogleAPIKey string
    
    // OpenAI
    OpenAIAPIKey      string
    OpenAIBaseURL     string
    OpenAISearchModel string // search-capable model for web search
}
```

**Provider selection logic**:
- **Gemini** (default): Uses Google's native SDK (`google.golang.org/genai`) with ADK's `gemini.NewModel()` and `geminitool.GoogleSearch`
- **OpenAI**: Uses custom adapter (`internal/agent/openai/`) implementing `model.LLM` interface with Web Search support

---

### 4. **OpenAI Adapter Implementation**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/openai.go`

The adapter provides **ADK-compatible OpenAI integration**:

- **`openaiModel` struct**: Implements `model.LLM` interface
- **`GenerateContent()`**: Converts ADK `LLMRequest` â†’ OpenAI chat completion â†’ ADK `LLMResponse`
- **Streaming**: Not implemented (always yields single response)
- **Tool conversion**: Transforms ADK `genai.Tool` definitions into OpenAI function schema
- **Web Search**: Detects `GoogleSearch` marker in request and emits `web_search_options` in HTTP body

**Example request conversion**:
```go
// OpenAI endpoint: POST /v1/chat/completions
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "messages": [...],  // ADK genai.Content â†’ OpenAI chat messages
  "tools": [...],     // ADK function declarations â†’ OpenAI function schema
  "web_search_options": {}  // enabled if search_agent is used
}
```

---

### 5. **Web Search Tool Setup**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`

The `WebSearch` tool is provider-specific:

```go
// OpenAI: custom WebSearch tool
searchTool = oaimodel.WebSearch{}

// Gemini: native GoogleSearch tool
searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
```

Both tools are registered with the search sub-agent:
```go
searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
    Name:        "search_agent",
    Description: "Search the web for additional context...",
    Model:       searchModel,
    Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},  // provider-specific
})
```

---

### 6. **Agent Orchestrator Lifecycle**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go`

The `Orchestrator` manages the complete agent run:

```go
type Orchestrator struct {
    store     OrchestratorStore
    llmConfig LLMConfig
    publicURL string
}
```

**`RunAgent()` workflow** (lines 224-254):
1. Mark run as "running"
2. Execute agent via `executeAgent()` (detailed below)
3. Parse JSON report from LLM output
4. Create `SemanticRelease` with linked `semantic_release_sources`
5. Send project-level notifications
6. Mark run as "completed"

**`executeAgent()` execution** (lines 267-427):
1. Load project config (custom `agent_prompt`)
2. Build dynamic instruction with version substitution (line 85)
3. Create LLM model via `NewLLMModel()` (supports Gemini or OpenAI)
4. Create project-scoped tools via `NewTools(store, projectID)`
5. Build multi-agent hierarchy:
   - Data agent (function tools)
   - Search agent (web search tool)
   - Root agent (agentools wrapping both)
6. Run via ADK `runner.Runner` with in-memory session
7. Capture final text response
8. Parse as JSON `SemanticReport`
9. Fallback: store raw text if parsing fails

---

### 7. **Agent Worker & River Job Integration**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/worker.go`

The `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`:

```go
func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {
    // 1. Load agent run
    run, err := w.store.GetAgentRun(ctx, job.Args.AgentRunID)
    
    // 2. Check multi-source waiting (if WaitForAllSources rule enabled)
    if run.Version != "" && rules.WaitForAllSources {
        ready, _ := w.orchestrator.checkAllSourcesReady(ctx, run.ProjectID, run.Version)
        if !ready && job.Attempt < 12 {
            return river.JobSnooze(5 * time.Minute)  // Retry in 5 min
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Execute agent
    return w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run)
}
```

**Timeout**: 5 minutes per job (accounts for multiple LLM round-trips)

---

### 8. **Job Queuing & Triggering**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/queue/jobs.go`

```go
type AgentJobArgs struct {
    AgentRunID string `json:"agent_run_id"`
    ProjectID  string `json:"project_id"`
    Version    string `json:"version"`
}

func (AgentJobArgs) Kind() string { return "agent_run" }
```

**Two triggering paths**:

1. **API Manual Trigger** (`/api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run`)
   - Files: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/agent.go` (lines 34-57)
   - Calls `store.TriggerAgentRun(projectID, trigger, version)`

2. **Auto-trigger on Release** (via NotifyWorker)
   - File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/routing/worker.go` (lines 80-150)
   - Checks project's `AgentRules` (on_major_release, on_security_patch, version_pattern, wait_for_all_sources)
   - Calls `store.EnqueueAgentRun()` if criteria match

**Transactional Outbox** (file: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/pgstore.go`, lines 892-914):
```go
func (s *PgStore) EnqueueAgentRun(ctx context.Context, projectID, trigger, version string) error {
    tx, _ := s.pool.Begin(ctx)
    // 1. INSERT agent_runs row
    tx.QueryRow(...INSERT INTO agent_runs...)
    // 2. INSERT River job in same transaction
    s.river.InsertTx(ctx, tx, queue.AgentJobArgs{...}, nil)
    // 3. Commit (zero-loss guarantee)
}
```

---

### 9. **LLM Configuration & Initialization**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/cmd/server/main.go` (lines 66-86)

Agent worker registration in main:
```go
llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    Provider:          llmProvider,
    Model:             envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    GoogleAPIKey:      os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    OpenAIAPIKey:      os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    OpenAIBaseURL:     os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    OpenAISearchModel: envOr("OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL", "gpt-5-search-api"),
}

agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
if err != nil {
    slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available â€” agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
} else {
    agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
}
```

**Deployment model**: Single binary with conditional agent worker registration based on API key availability.

---

### 10. **Agent Development CLI**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/cmd/agent/main.go`

A standalone CLI tool for interactive agent testing with ADK-Web UI:

```bash
go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> [web]
```

Uses `launcher.NewLauncher()` to start an interactive web UI for agent debugging and testing.

---

### 11. **Default Agent Instruction & Output Schema**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go` (lines 46-75)

The agent is instructed to output a **JSON `SemanticReport`** with these fields:

```json
{
  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level...",
  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes...",
  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation...",
  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation...",
  "download_commands": ["docker pull ..."],
  "download_links": ["https://..."]
}
```

The instruction includes version substitution: `{{VERSION}}` â†’ actual target version.

---

### 12. **Semantic Release Model & Persistence**

**File**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/models/semantic_release.go`

```go
type SemanticReport struct {
    Subject          string   `json:"subject"`
    Urgency          string   `json:"urgency"`
    UrgencyReason    string   `json:"urgency_reason,omitempty"`
    StatusChecks     []string `json:"status_checks"`
    ChangelogSummary string   `json:"changelog_summary"`
    // ... plus legacy fields for backward compat
}

type SemanticRelease struct {
    ID          string          `json:"id"`
    ProjectID   string          `json:"project_id"`
    Version     string          `json:"version"`
    Report      json.RawMessage `json:"report,omitempty"`  // SemanticReport as JSONB
    Status      string          `json:"status"`  // pending, processing, completed, failed
    CompletedAt *time.Time      `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
}
```

**Persistence** (orchestrator.go, lines 379-413):
- Gathers all releases matching target version
- Creates `semantic_release_sources` join table entries
- Stores full report as JSONB
- Links to agent run via `UpdateAgentRunResult()`

---

### 13. **Key Data Interfaces & Store Contracts**

**Agent Data Access** (`tools.go`, line 15):
```go
type AgentDataStore interface {
    ListReleasesByProject(ctx, projectID, page, perPage, includeExcluded)
    GetRelease(ctx, id)
    ListContextSources(ctx, projectID, page, perPage)
}
```

**Orchestrator Store** (`orchestrator.go`, lines 33-44):
```go
type OrchestratorStore interface {
    AgentDataStore
    GetProject(ctx, id)
    GetAgentRun(ctx, id)
    UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, id, status)
    CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs)
    UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, id, semanticReleaseID)
    ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, projectID)
    GetChannel(ctx, id)
    ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, page, perPage)
    HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, sourceID, version)
}
```

All implemented by `api.PgStore`.

---

### 14. **Graceful Degradation**

The system gracefully degrades when LLM API keys are unavailable:

1. **Server startup** (main.go, line 79-86):
   - `NewOrchestrator()` returns error if API key missing
   - Agent worker is NOT registered
   - Rest of system (ingestion, notifications) continues

2. **At runtime**:
   - Agent jobs remain in River queue unprocessed
   - Restart server with API key configured to resume

3. **Agent run failure**:
   - Marked as "failed" with error captured
   - JSON parse failure: falls back to storing raw text as summary

---

### Summary of Key Files

| File Path | Purpose | Lines |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/tools.go` | ADK function tool definitions (get_releases, get_release_detail, list_context_sources) | 94-220 |

| `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/routing/worker.go` | Agent rule checking & auto-trigger logic | 80-150 |

    74â†’  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    75â†’}`

    77â†’// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    78â†’// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    79â†’// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    80â†’func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    81â†’	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    82â†’	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    83â†’		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    84â†’	}
    85â†’	instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    86â†’
    87â†’	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig)
    88â†’	if err != nil {
    89â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    90â†’	}
    91â†’
    92â†’	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    93â†’	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    94â†’	if err != nil {
    95â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    96â†’	}
    97â†’
    98â†’	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
    99â†’	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   100â†’		Name:        "data_agent",
   101â†’		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   102â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   103â†’		Tools:       functionTools,
   104â†’	})
   105â†’	if err != nil {
   106â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   107â†’	}
   108â†’
   109â†’	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   110â†’	var searchTool tool.Tool
   111â†’	var searchModel model.LLM
   112â†’	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   113â†’	case "openai":
   114â†’		searchTool = oaimodel.WebSearch{}
   115â†’		// OpenAI web search requires a search-capable model.
   116â†’		searchModelName := llmConfig.OpenAISearchModel
   117â†’		if searchModelName == "" {
   118â†’			searchModelName = "gpt-5-search-api"
   119â†’		}
   120â†’		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, LLMConfig{
   121â†’			Provider:      "openai",
   122â†’			Model:         searchModelName,
   123â†’			OpenAIAPIKey:  llmConfig.OpenAIAPIKey,
   124â†’			OpenAIBaseURL: llmConfig.OpenAIBaseURL,
   125â†’		})
   126â†’		if err != nil {
   127â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   128â†’		}
   129â†’	default: // gemini
   130â†’		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   131â†’		searchModel = llmModel
   132â†’	}
   133â†’
   134â†’	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   135â†’		Name:        "search_agent",
   136â†’		Description: "Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues.",
   137â†’		Model:       searchModel,
   138â†’		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   139â†’	})
   140â†’	if err != nil {
   141â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   142â†’	}
   143â†’
   144â†’	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   145â†’	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   146â†’		Name:        "release_analyst",
   147â†’		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   148â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   149â†’		Instruction: instruction,
   150â†’		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   151â†’			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   152â†’			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   153â†’		},
   154â†’	})
   155â†’}

   157â†’// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   158â†’// release matching the target version.
   159â†’func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   160â†’	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   161â†’	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   162â†’	if err != nil {
   163â†’		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   164â†’	}
   165â†’	if len(sources) == 0 {
   166â†’		return true, nil
   167â†’	}
   168â†’
   169â†’	for _, src := range sources {
   170â†’		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   171â†’		if err != nil {
   172â†’			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   173â†’		}
   174â†’		if !has {
   175â†’			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   176â†’				"project_id", projectID,
   177â†’				"source_id", src.ID,
   178â†’				"version", version,
   179â†’			)
   180â†’			return false, nil
   181â†’		}
   182â†’	}
   183â†’	return true, nil
   184â†’}
