   186â†’// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   187â†’// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   188â†’// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   189â†’type Orchestrator struct {
   190â†’	store     OrchestratorStore
   191â†’	llmConfig LLMConfig
   192â†’	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   193â†’}
   194â†’
   195â†’// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   196â†’// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   197â†’// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   198â†’func NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   199â†’	switch cfg.Provider {
   200â†’	case "openai":
   201â†’		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   202â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   203â†’		}
   204â†’	default: // gemini
   205â†’		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   206â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   207â†’		}
   208â†’	}
   209â†’	return &Orchestrator{
   210â†’		store:     store,
   211â†’		llmConfig: cfg,
   212â†’		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   213â†’	}, nil
   214â†’}
   215â†’
   216â†’// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   217â†’//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   218â†’//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   219â†’//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   220â†’//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   221â†’//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   222â†’//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   223â†’//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   224â†’func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   225â†’	// Mark as running.
   226â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   227â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   228â†’	}
   229â†’
   230â†’	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   231â†’	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   232â†’	if err != nil {
   233â†’		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   234â†’		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   235â†’		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   236â†’			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   237â†’				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   238â†’		}
   239â†’		return err
   240â†’	}
   241â†’
   242â†’	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   243â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   244â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   245â†’	}
   246â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   247â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   248â†’	}
   249â†’
   250â†’	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   251â†’	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   252â†’
   253â†’	return nil
   254â†’}
   255â†’
   256â†’// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   257â†’// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   258â†’type agentResult struct {
   259â†’	semanticReleaseID string
   260â†’	version           string
   261â†’	reportText        string
   262â†’	projectName       string
   263â†’}
   264â†’
   265â†’// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   266â†’// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   267â†’func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   268â†’	// Load project.
   269â†’	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   270â†’	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   271â†’	if err != nil {
   272â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   273â†’	}
   274â†’	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   275â†’
   276â†’	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   277â†’	version := run.Version
   278â†’	if version == "" {
   279â†’		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   280â†’		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   281â†’			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   282â†’		}
   283â†’	}
   284â†’
   285â†’	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   286â†’	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   287â†’		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   288â†’		"version", version)
   289â†’	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   290â†’	if err != nil {
   291â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   292â†’	}
   293â†’
   294â†’	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   295â†’	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   296â†’	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   297â†’		AppName: "changelogue",
   298â†’		UserID:  "system",
   299â†’	})
   300â†’	if err != nil {
   301â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   302â†’	}
   303â†’	sess := createResp.Session
   304â†’
   305â†’	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   306â†’	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   307â†’		AppName:        "changelogue",
   308â†’		Agent:          agentInstance,
   309â†’		SessionService: sessionService,
   310â†’		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   311â†’			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   312â†’			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   313â†’		},
   314â†’	})
   315â†’	if err != nil {
   316â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   317â†’	}
   318â†’
   319â†’	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   320â†’	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   321â†’	var userPrompt string
   322â†’	if version != "" {
   323â†’		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   324â†’	} else {
   325â†’		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   326â†’	}
   327â†’	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   328â†’
   329â†’	var finalText string
   330â†’	eventCount := 0
   331â†’	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   332â†’		if err != nil {
   333â†’			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   334â†’				"run_id", run.ID,
   335â†’				"event_count", eventCount,
   336â†’				"err", err,
   337â†’			)
   338â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   339â†’		}
   340â†’		eventCount++
   341â†’		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   342â†’			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   343â†’				if part.Text != "" {
   344â†’					finalText += part.Text
   345â†’				}
   346â†’			}
   347â†’		}
   348â†’	}
   349â†’	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   350â†’		"run_id", run.ID,
   351â†’		"event_count", eventCount,
   352â†’		"output_length", len(finalText),
   353â†’	)
   354â†’
   355â†’	if finalText == "" {
   356â†’		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   357â†’			"run_id", run.ID,
   358â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   359â†’			"event_count", eventCount,
   360â†’		)
   361â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   362â†’	}
   363â†’
   364â†’	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   365â†’	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   366â†’	if err != nil {
   367â†’		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   368â†’			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   369â†’		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   370â†’		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   371â†’			Summary: finalText,
   372â†’		}
   373â†’	}
   374â†’
   375â†’	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   376â†’	if err != nil {
   377â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   378â†’	}
   379â†’
   380â†’	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   381â†’	// Only link releases that match the target version.
   382â†’	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 200, false)
   383â†’	if err != nil {
   384â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   385â†’	}
   386â†’	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   387â†’	for _, r := range releases {
   388â†’		if r.Version == version {
   389â†’			releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   390â†’		}
   391â†’	}
   392â†’
   393â†’	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   394â†’	srVersion := version
   395â†’	if srVersion == "" {
   396â†’		srVersion = "unknown"
   397â†’		if len(releases) > 0 {
   398â†’			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   399â†’		}
   400â†’	}
   401â†’
   402â†’	now := time.Now()
   403â†’	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   404â†’		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   405â†’		Version:     srVersion,
   406â†’		Report:      reportJSON,
   407â†’		Status:      "completed",
   408â†’		CompletedAt: &now,
   409â†’	}
   410â†’
   411â†’	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   412â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   413â†’	}
   414â†’
   415â†’	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   416â†’		"run_id", run.ID,
   417â†’		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   418â†’		"version", sr.Version,
   419â†’	)
   420â†’
   421â†’	return &agentResult{
   422â†’		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   423â†’		version:           srVersion,
   424â†’		reportText:        finalText,
   425â†’		projectName:       project.Name,
   426â†’	}, nil
   427â†’}
   428â†’
   429â†’// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   430â†’// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   431â†’func defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   432â†’	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   433â†’	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   434â†’		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   435â†’		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   436â†’		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   437â†’	}
   438â†’}
   439â†’
   440â†’// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   441â†’// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   442â†’// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   443â†’func (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   444â†’	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   445â†’	if err != nil {
   446â†’		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   447â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   448â†’		return
   449â†’	}
   450â†’	if len(subs) == 0 {
   451â†’		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   452â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   453â†’		return
   454â†’	}
   455â†’
   456â†’	senders := defaultSenders()
   457â†’
   458â†’	msg := routing.Notification{
   459â†’		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   460â†’		Body:        result.reportText,
   461â†’		Version:     result.version,
   462â†’		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   463â†’	}
   464â†’
   465â†’	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   466â†’	if o.publicURL != "" {
   467â†’		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   468â†’	}
   469â†’
   470â†’	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   471â†’	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   472â†’	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   473â†’		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   474â†’		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   475â†’		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   476â†’	}
   477â†’
   478â†’	for _, sub := range subs {
   479â†’		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   480â†’		if err != nil {
   481â†’			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   482â†’				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   483â†’			continue
   484â†’		}
   485â†’
   486â†’		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   487â†’		if !ok {
   488â†’			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   489â†’			continue
   490â†’		}
   491â†’
   492â†’		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   493â†’			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   494â†’				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   495â†’		} else {
   496â†’			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   497â†’				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   498â†’		}
   499â†’	}
   500â†’}

   502â†’// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   503â†’// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   504â†’func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   505â†’	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   506â†’	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   507â†’	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   508â†’		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   509â†’		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   510â†’			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   511â†’		}
   512â†’		// Remove closing fence.
   513â†’		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   514â†’			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   515â†’		}
   516â†’		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   517â†’	}
   518â†’
   519â†’	var report models.SemanticReport
   520â†’	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   521â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   522â†’	}
   523â†’
   524â†’	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   525â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   526â†’	}
   527â†’
   528â†’	return &report, nil
   529â†’}

content.text:
Now I have a good picture of the current state. Let me ask a clarifying question.

I see that you already have an OpenAI web search integration, but it uses the **Chat Completions API** with `web_search_options: {}` and a dedicated search model (`gpt-5-search-api`). content.text:
Good. So the goal is to migrate the OpenAI adapter from the **Chat Completions API** to the **Responses API**, gaining access to the proper `web_search` tool with parameters like domain filtering, search context size, and user location.

Here are the approaches I see:

## Approach 1: Full Responses API migration (Recommended)

Replace the entire `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` adapter to call `/v1/responses` instead of `/v1/chat/completions`. This means:

- New request/response types matching the Responses API format (`input`, `tools: [{type: "web_search", ...}]`, output items)
- The `WebSearch` tool can now carry config (allowed_domains, search_context_size)
- No more need for a separate `OpenAISearchModel` â€” the same model handles search natively
- **Trade-off**: Larger change, touches the core adapter. The Responses API uses a different message format (`input` items vs `messages` array). But it's a clean break.

## Approach 2: Hybrid â€” add Responses API alongside Chat Completions

Keep the existing Chat Completions adapter for the main model, but add a second adapter that uses the Responses API specifically for web search calls. The search sub-agent would use the Responses API adapter.

- **Trade-off**: Two code paths to maintain, but lower risk since the main agent path is unchanged.

## Approach 3: Chat Completions with enhanced web_search_options

Stay on Chat Completions but enhance `web_search_options` with the parameters it does support (it supports `search_context_size` and `user_location` even on Chat Completions).

- **Trade-off**: Minimal change, but Chat Completions web search only works with special `-search-` models and has fewer features than the Responses API. Doesn't give you domain filtering.

**My recommendation is Approach 1** â€” a full migration to the Responses API. The Responses API is OpenAI's primary path forward, supports all models (no special search model needed), and gives full control over web search parameters. The Chat Completions search models are legacy. This also simplifies `LLMConfig` by removing `OpenAISearchModel`.

content.text:
## Design: Migrate OpenAI Adapter to Responses API

### Architecture

The core change is replacing `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `POST /v1/responses`. This changes the wire format but the ADK interface (`model.LLM`) stays the same.

**Request format changes:**
- `messages` array â†’ `input` (string or array of input items)
- `tools: [{type: "function", function: {...}}]` â†’ `tools: [{type: "function", ...}, {type: "web_search", ...}]`
- `web_search_options: {}` (separate field) â†’ `tools: [{type: "web_search", search_context_size: "medium", user_location: {...}}]` (native tool)

**Response format changes:**
- `choices[0].message.content` â†’ `output` array with `message` items
- `choices[0].message.tool_calls` â†’ `output` items with `type: "function_call"`
- Annotations (citations) come embedded in `output_text.annotations`

### Key Components

1. **`openai.go`** â€” Rewrite `doRequest()` and all type definitions to use `/v1/responses` format
2. **`websearch.go`** â€” `WebSearch` tool now carries optional config (allowed_domains, search_context_size) that gets encoded directly into the `tools` array
3. **`model.go`** â€” Remove `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` since any model can now do web search
4. **`orchestrator.go`** â€” Simplify search sub-agent creation (no separate search model needed)
5. **`cmd/server/main.go`** â€” Remove `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var

### WebSearch Tool Config

```go
type WebSearch struct {
    SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
    AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter
}
```

### Simplification

The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model. When `provider=openai`, both the data agent and search agent can use the same model â€” web search is just another tool in the `tools` array. This eliminates the `OpenAISearchModel` config and the second `NewLLMModel()` call in `BuildAgent`.

Does this design look right so far?

# Design: Migrate OpenAI Adapter to Responses API

**Date:** 2026-03-04
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

The current OpenAI adapter uses the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) with `web_search_options: {}` for web search. This has limitations:

- Requires a dedicated search-capable model (`gpt-5-search-api`) â€” separate from the main model
- No control over search parameters (domain filtering, search context size, user location)
- Chat Completions search models are being superseded by the Responses API

## Solution

Migrate the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`), which:

- Supports `web_search` as a native tool alongside function tools
- Works with any supported model (gpt-5, gpt-4.1, o4-mini, etc.)
- Provides domain filtering (`allowed_domains`), `search_context_size`, and `user_location`
- Eliminates the need for a separate search model

## Scope

### Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` | Rewrite request/response types and `doRequest()` for Responses API |
| `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go` | Update tests for new wire format |
| `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go` | Add configurable fields (SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains) |
| `internal/agent/model.go` | Remove `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` |
| `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` | Simplify search sub-agent (same model, no separate model creation) |
| `cmd/server/main.go` | Remove `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var |

### Request Format (Before â†’ After)

**Before (Chat Completions):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_releases", ...}}],
  "web_search_options": {}
}
```

**After (Responses):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "input": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [
    {"type": "function", "name": "get_releases", "parameters": {...}},
    {"type": "web_search", "search_context_size": "medium"}
  ]
}
```

### Response Format (Before â†’ After)

**Before:** `choices[0].message.content` + `choices[0].message.tool_calls`

**After:** `output` array containing:
- `{type: "web_search_call", status: "completed"}` â€” search invocation record
- `{type: "function_call", id, name, arguments}` â€” function tool calls
- `{type: "message", content: [{type: "output_text", text, annotations}]}` â€” final text with citations

### WebSearch Tool Enhancement

```go
type WebSearch struct {
    SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
    AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter
}
```

The `ProcessRequest` method will encode these fields into the tool config rather than using the GoogleSearch marker hack.

### Config Simplification

**Removed from LLMConfig:**
- `OpenAISearchModel` â€” no longer needed; any model supports web search via tools

**Removed env vars:**
- `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` â€” search model is the same as the main model

### Orchestrator Simplification

The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model. In `BuildAgent()`:

```go
// Before: separate model for search
searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, LLMConfig{
    Provider: "openai", Model: "gpt-5-search-api", ...
})

// After: same model, web search is just a tool
searchAgent = llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
    Model: llmModel,  // same model as root/data agent
    Tools: []tool.Tool{oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}},
})
```

## Non-Goals

- Streaming support (not implemented today, stays that way)
- Deep research models (o3-deep-research etc.)
- Gemini adapter changes (unaffected)

Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

**Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

**Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

**Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

**Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (this session)** - I dispatch fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Parallel Session (separate)** - Open new session with executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Stay in this session
- Fresh subagent per task + code review

**If Parallel Session chosen:**
- Guide them to open new session in worktree
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** New session uses superpowers:executing-plans

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan.

Let me first check existing tests and understand the exact Responses API format more deeply before writing the plan.

I need the EXACT JSON request and response format for the OpenAI Responses API. Please provide:

1. The exact request body structure for POST /v1/responses - all fields, especially:

   - How function tools are defined in the tools array (exact schema)
   - How web_search tool is defined (with search_context_size, user_location, allowed_domains)
   - How to include previous conversation (with tool call results)
   - The "previous_response_id" for multi-turn

2. The exact response body structure - all fields, especially:
   - The output array item types (message, function_call, web_search_call, etc.)
   - How function_call items look (id, name, arguments, call_id)

content.text: Now I have everything I need. Let me also quickly check the Responses API reference for the exact function tool format differences.

input.prompt:
Show me the exact format for sending function call results back in the Responses API multi-turn flow. Specifically:

1. When the model returns function_call items, how do I send results back? Do I need to echo the function_call item AND add a function_call_output, or just the output?
2. Is there a "previous_response_id" approach that avoids re-sending the full history?
3. What is the exact format of the function_call_output input item?

Also confirm: in the Responses API function tool definition, is `description` nested inside a `function` key or at the top level alongside `type` and `name`?

content.content:
## Responses API Function Calling: Multi-Turn Format

### 1. Echoing function_call items + adding outputs

Yes, you need **both**. The docs show appending the entire `response.output` (which includes function_call items), then adding output items:

```python
input_list += response.output

for item in response.output:
    if item.type == "function_call":
        input_list.append({
            "type": "function_call_output",
            "call_id": item.call_id,
            "output": json.dumps({"horoscope": horoscope})
        })
```

The pattern is: accumulate a running input list, append all output items from the response, then append your `function_call_output` entries matched by `call_id`.

### 2. `previous_response_id` approach

The page content provided does not mention a `previous_response_id` parameter for the Responses API function calling flow. The documented approach uses a "running input list" that you build up over time.

### 3. Exact format of `function_call_output`

```json
{
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_12345xyz",
    "output": "{\"temperature\": \"25\", \"unit\": \"C\"}"
}
```

The `output` field is typically a string (JSON-encoded or plain text). For functions returning images/files, you can pass "an array of image or file objects instead of a string."

### 4. Tool definition structure in Responses API

In the Responses API, `description` is at the **top level** alongside `type` and `name` â€” there is **no** nested `function` key. Compare:

**Responses API format:**
```json
{
    "type": "function",
    "name": "get_horoscope",
    "description": "Get today's horoscope for an astrological sign.",
    "parameters": { ... }
}
```

**Chat Completions API format** (different â€” has nested `function` key):
```json
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_horoscope",
        "description": "Get today's horoscope...",
        "parameters": { ... },
        "strict": true
    }
}
```

This is a key structural difference between the two APIs.

content.text: Excellent. Now I have the complete picture. Let me also check one critical detail â€” in the Responses API, when ADK calls `GenerateContent` in a multi-turn loop, how does it pass conversation history? Let me look at how ADK sends the request.

Now I understand the full picture. ADK passes `req.Contents` (the conversation history) and `req.Config` (system instruction, tools, generation params) to `GenerateContent`. The adapter needs to convert these to the Responses API format.

Key difference: In the Responses API multi-turn flow, function_call items from the model's output must be echoed back as input items before the function_call_output. The ADK already handles this â€” it sends FunctionCall parts followed by FunctionResponse parts in `req.Contents`. We just need to map them to the Responses API format.

Let me write the implementation plan now.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: âś… Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: âťŚ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: âś… Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: âś… Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is âś…** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

Let me read the plan and set up the task list.

1â†’# OpenAI Responses API Migration â€” Implementation Plan
     2â†’
     3â†’> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.
     4â†’
     5â†’**Goal:** Migrate the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions API to Responses API for native web search tool support with domain filtering and search context control.
     6â†’
     7â†’**Architecture:** Replace `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `POST /v1/responses` in the OpenAI adapter. The Responses API uses a different wire format (`input` items instead of `messages`, flat tool definitions instead of nested `function` wrappers, `function_call`/`function_call_output` input items instead of `tool` role messages). The WebSearch tool gains config fields (SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains). The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model.
     8â†’
     9â†’**Tech Stack:** Go, `net/http`, Google ADK-Go v0.5.0 (`model.LLM` interface)
    10â†’
    11â†’**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-03-04-openai-responses-api-design.md`
    12â†’
    13â†’---
    14â†’
    15â†’### Task 1: Rewrite WebSearch tool with config fields
    16â†’
    17â†’**Files:**
    18â†’- Modify: `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`
    19â†’- Modify: `internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go`
    20â†’
    21â†’**Step 1: Write the failing tests**
    22â†’
    23â†’Update `websearch_test.go` to test that WebSearch carries config and ProcessRequest adds a `webSearchConfig` marker to the request (instead of GoogleSearch marker):
    24â†’
    25â†’```go
    26â†’package openai
    27â†’
    28â†’import (
    29â†’	"testing"
    30â†’
    31â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
    32â†’)
    33â†’
    34â†’func TestWebSearch_Name(t *testing.T) {
    35â†’	ws := WebSearch{}
    36â†’	if ws.Name() != "web_search" {
    37â†’		t.Errorf("expected name 'web_search', got %q", ws.Name())
    38â†’	}
    39â†’}
    40â†’
    41â†’func TestWebSearch_IsLongRunning(t *testing.T) {
    42â†’	ws := WebSearch{}
    43â†’	if ws.IsLongRunning() {
    44â†’		t.Error("expected IsLongRunning to return false")
    45â†’	}
    46â†’}
    47â†’
    48â†’func TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_SetsMarker(t *testing.T) {
    49â†’	ws := WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "high"}
    50â†’	req := &model.LLMRequest{}
    51â†’
    52â†’	if err := ws.ProcessRequest(nil, req); err != nil {
    53â†’		t.Fatalf("ProcessRequest error: %v", err)
    54â†’	}
    55â†’
    56â†’	if req.Config == nil {
    57â†’		t.Fatal("expected Config to be set")
    58â†’	}
    59â†’	// The marker is still a GoogleSearch tool (ADK convention), but the
    60â†’	// OpenAI adapter reads our webSearchConfig from the model wrapper.
    61â†’	if len(req.Config.Tools) != 1 {
    62â†’		t.Fatalf("expected 1 tool, got %d", len(req.Config.Tools))
    63â†’	}
    64â†’	if req.Config.Tools[0].GoogleSearch == nil {
    65â†’		t.Error("expected GoogleSearch marker to be set")
    66â†’	}
    67â†’}
    68â†’
    69â†’func TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_NilRequest(t *testing.T) {
    70â†’	ws := WebSearch{}
    71â†’	err := ws.ProcessRequest(nil, nil)
    72â†’	if err == nil {
    73â†’		t.Error("expected error for nil request")
    74â†’	}
    75â†’}
    76â†’```
    77â†’
    78â†’**Step 2: Run tests to verify they pass (marker behavior is unchanged)**
    79â†’
    80â†’Run: `go test -v -run TestWebSearch ./internal/agent/openai/...`
    81â†’Expected: PASS â€” the marker behavior hasn't changed yet, we just added config fields.
    82â†’
    83â†’**Step 3: Update WebSearch struct with config fields**
    84â†’
    85â†’In `websearch.go`, add the configurable fields:
    86â†’
    87â†’```go
    88â†’package openai
    89â†’
    90â†’import (
    91â†’	"fmt"
    92â†’
    93â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
    94â†’
    95â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
    96â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    97â†’)
    98â†’
    99â†’// WebSearch is a tool that enables OpenAI's built-in web search via the
   100â†’// Responses API tools array. It adds a GoogleSearch marker to the ADK
   101â†’// request config, which the OpenAI adapter translates into a
   102â†’// {"type": "web_search", ...} tool entry.
   103â†’type WebSearch struct {
   104â†’	SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
   105â†’	AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter (up to 100)
   106â†’}
   107â†’
   108â†’// Name implements tool.Tool.
   109â†’func (WebSearch) Name() string { return "web_search" }
   110â†’
   111â†’// Description implements tool.Tool.
   112â†’func (WebSearch) Description() string {
   113â†’	return "Search the web for additional context about a release."
   114â†’}
   115â†’
   116â†’// IsLongRunning implements tool.Tool.
   117â†’func (WebSearch) IsLongRunning() bool { return false }
   118â†’
   119â†’// ProcessRequest implements toolinternal.RequestProcessor by adding a
   120â†’// GoogleSearch marker to the LLM request. The OpenAI model wrapper
   121â†’// detects this marker and emits a web_search tool in the Responses API body.
   122â†’func (ws WebSearch) ProcessRequest(_ tool.Context, req *model.LLMRequest) error {
   123â†’	if req == nil {
   124â†’		return fmt.Errorf("llm request is nil")
   125â†’	}
   126â†’	if req.Config == nil {
   127â†’		req.Config = &genai.GenerateContentConfig{}
   128â†’	}
   129â†’	req.Config.Tools = append(req.Config.Tools, &genai.Tool{
   130â†’		GoogleSearch: &genai.GoogleSearch{},
   131â†’	})
   132â†’	return nil
   133â†’}
   134â†’```
   135â†’
   136â†’**Step 4: Run tests**
   137â†’
   138â†’Run: `go test -v -run TestWebSearch ./internal/agent/openai/...`
   139â†’Expected: PASS
   140â†’
   141â†’**Step 5: Commit**
   142â†’
   143â†’```bash
   144â†’git add internal/agent/openai/websearch.go internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go
   145â†’git commit -m "feat(agent/openai): add config fields to WebSearch tool"
   146â†’```
   147â†’
   148â†’---
   149â†’
   150â†’### Task 2: Rewrite OpenAI adapter request types for Responses API
   151â†’
   152â†’**Files:**
   153â†’- Modify: `internal/agent/openai/openai.go`
   154â†’
   155â†’**Step 1: Replace Chat Completions types with Responses API types**
   156â†’
   157â†’Replace the type definitions in `openai.go`. Remove `chatRequest`, `chatMessage`, `chatTool`, `chatFunction`, `chatResponse`, `chatChoice`, `chatUsage`, `apiError`, `webSearchOptions`, `toolCall`, `functionCall`.
   158â†’
   159â†’Add new types:
   160â†’
   161â†’```go
   162â†’// --- Responses API types ---
   163â†’
   164â†’// responsesRequest is the body for POST /v1/responses.
   165â†’type responsesRequest struct {
   166â†’	Model        string         `json:"model"`
   167â†’	Input        []inputItem    `json:"input"`
   168â†’	Instructions string         `json:"instructions,omitempty"`
   169â†’	Tools        []responseTool `json:"tools,omitempty"`
   170â†’	Temperature  *float32       `json:"temperature,omitempty"`
   171â†’	TopP         *float32       `json:"top_p,omitempty"`
   172â†’	MaxTokens    int32          `json:"max_output_tokens,omitempty"`
   173â†’	Store        bool           `json:"store"`
   174â†’}
   175â†’
   176â†’// inputItem is a union type for input array entries.
   177â†’// Only one of the "type" variants is populated per item.
   178â†’type inputItem struct {
   179â†’	// Message input (role + content)
   180â†’	Type    string `json:"type"`              // "message", "function_call", "function_call_output"
   181â†’	Role    string `json:"role,omitempty"`     // "user", "assistant", "system", "developer"
   182â†’	Content string `json:"content,omitempty"` // for message and function_call_output
   183â†’
   184â†’	// function_call echo fields
   185â†’	ID        string `json:"id,omitempty"`
   186â†’	CallID    string `json:"call_id,omitempty"`
   187â†’	Name      string `json:"name,omitempty"`
   188â†’	Arguments string `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
   189â†’
   190â†’	// function_call_output fields
   191â†’	// CallID and Content are reused
   192â†’	Output string `json:"output,omitempty"` // function result
   193â†’}
   194â†’
   195â†’// responseTool represents a tool in the Responses API tools array.
   196â†’type responseTool struct {
   197â†’	Type string `json:"type"` // "function" or "web_search"
   198â†’
   199â†’	// Function tool fields (type=function)
   200â†’	Name        string `json:"name,omitempty"`
   201â†’	Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`
   202â†’	Parameters  any    `json:"parameters,omitempty"`
   203â†’
   204â†’	// Web search fields (type=web_search)
   205â†’	SearchContextSize string         `json:"search_context_size,omitempty"`
   206â†’	UserLocation      any            `json:"user_location,omitempty"`
   207â†’	Filters           *searchFilters `json:"filters,omitempty"`
   208â†’}
   209â†’
   210â†’type searchFilters struct {
   211â†’	AllowedDomains []string `json:"allowed_domains,omitempty"`
   212â†’}
   213â†’
   214â†’// responsesResponse is the top-level response from POST /v1/responses.
   215â†’type responsesResponse struct {
   216â†’	ID         string       `json:"id"`
   217â†’	Status     string       `json:"status"`
   218â†’	Output     []outputItem `json:"output"`
   219â†’	OutputText string       `json:"output_text"`
   220â†’	Usage      *respUsage   `json:"usage,omitempty"`
   221â†’	Error      *apiError    `json:"error,omitempty"`
   222â†’}
   223â†’
   224â†’// outputItem is a union type for output array entries.
   225â†’type outputItem struct {
   226â†’	Type   string `json:"type"` // "message", "function_call", "web_search_call"
   227â†’	ID     string `json:"id,omitempty"`
   228â†’	Status string `json:"status,omitempty"`
   229â†’
   230â†’	// function_call fields
   231â†’	CallID    string `json:"call_id,omitempty"`
   232â†’	Name      string `json:"name,omitempty"`
   233â†’	Arguments string `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
   234â†’
   235â†’	// message fields
   236â†’	Role    string          `json:"role,omitempty"`
   237â†’	Content []outputContent `json:"content,omitempty"`
   238â†’}
   239â†’
   240â†’type outputContent struct {
   241â†’	Type        string       `json:"type"` // "output_text"
   242â†’	Text        string       `json:"text"`
   243â†’	Annotations []annotation `json:"annotations,omitempty"`
   244â†’}
   245â†’
   246â†’type annotation struct {
   247â†’	Type       string `json:"type"` // "url_citation"
   248â†’	URL        string `json:"url"`
   249â†’	Title      string `json:"title"`
   250â†’	StartIndex int    `json:"start_index"`
   251â†’	EndIndex   int    `json:"end_index"`
   252â†’}
   253â†’
   254â†’type respUsage struct {
   255â†’	InputTokens  int32 `json:"input_tokens"`
   256â†’	OutputTokens int32 `json:"output_tokens"`
   257â†’	TotalTokens  int32 `json:"total_tokens"`
   258â†’}
   259â†’
   260â†’type apiError struct {
   261â†’	Message string `json:"message"`
   262â†’	Type    string `json:"type"`
   263â†’	Code    string `json:"code"`
   264â†’}
   265â†’```
   266â†’
   267â†’**Step 2: Verify compilation**
   268â†’
   269â†’Run: `go build ./internal/agent/openai/...`
   270â†’Expected: compilation errors because `doRequest`, `convertContents`, `convertTools`, `convertResponse` still reference old types. This is expected â€” we fix them in Task 3.
   271â†’
   272â†’---
   273â†’
   274â†’### Task 3: Rewrite conversion functions for Responses API
   275â†’
   276â†’**Files:**
   277â†’- Modify: `internal/agent/openai/openai.go`
   278â†’
   279â†’**Step 1: Write tests for the new conversion functions**
   280â†’
   281â†’Add to `openai_test.go`:
   282â†’
   283â†’```go
   284â†’func TestConvertInputs_SystemInstruction(t *testing.T) {
   285â†’	req := &model.LLMRequest{
   286â†’		Config: &genai.GenerateContentConfig{
   287â†’			SystemInstruction: genai.NewContentFromText("You are helpful.", "system"),
   288â†’		},
   289â†’	}
   290â†’	_, instructions := convertInputs(req)
   291â†’	if instructions != "You are helpful." {
   292â†’		t.Errorf("expected instructions 'You are helpful.', got %q", instructions)
   293â†’	}
   294â†’}
   295â†’
   296â†’func TestConvertInputs_FunctionCallAndResponse(t *testing.T) {

   451â†’```go
   452â†’// convertInputs transforms ADK genai.Content messages into Responses API input items.
   453â†’// Returns the input items and extracted system instructions.
   454â†’func convertInputs(req *model.LLMRequest) ([]inputItem, string) {
   455â†’	var items []inputItem
   456â†’	var instructions string
   457â†’
   458â†’	// Extract system instruction separately (Responses API uses "instructions" field).
   459â†’	if req.Config != nil && req.Config.SystemInstruction != nil {
   460â†’		var text string
   461â†’		for _, p := range req.Config.SystemInstruction.Parts {
   462â†’			if p.Text != "" {
   463â†’				text += p.Text
   464â†’			}
   465â†’		}
   466â†’		instructions = text
   467â†’	}
   468â†’
   469â†’	// Conversation contents.
   470â†’	for _, c := range req.Contents {
   471â†’		role := mapRole(c.Role)
   472â†’
   473â†’		for _, p := range c.Parts {
   474â†’			switch {
   475â†’			case p.FunctionCall != nil:
   476â†’				argsJSON, _ := json.Marshal(p.FunctionCall.Args)
   477â†’				items = append(items, inputItem{
   478â†’					Type:      "function_call",
   479â†’					CallID:    p.FunctionCall.ID,
   480â†’					Name:      p.FunctionCall.Name,
   481â†’					Arguments: string(argsJSON),
   482â†’				})
   483â†’			case p.FunctionResponse != nil:
   484â†’				respJSON, _ := json.Marshal(p.FunctionResponse.Response)
   485â†’				items = append(items, inputItem{
   486â†’					Type:   "function_call_output",
   487â†’					CallID: p.FunctionResponse.ID,
   488â†’					Output: string(respJSON),
   489â†’				})
   490â†’			case p.Text != "":
   491â†’				items = append(items, inputItem{
   492â†’					Type:    "message",
   493â†’					Role:    role,
   494â†’					Content: p.Text,
   495â†’				})
   496â†’			}
   497â†’		}
   498â†’	}
   499â†’
   500â†’	return items, instructions
   501â†’}

   503â†’// webSearchConfig extracts web search parameters from the WebSearch tool
   504â†’// registered on the model. Returns nil if no WebSearch is configured.
   505â†’func (m *openaiModel) webSearchConfig() *WebSearch {
   506â†’	return m.wsConfig
   507â†’}
   508â†’
   509â†’// convertResponseTools transforms ADK genai.Tool definitions into Responses API tools.
   510â†’// If wsConfig is non-nil and a GoogleSearch marker is present, it emits a web_search
   511â†’// tool with the configured parameters.
   512â†’func convertResponseTools(req *model.LLMRequest, wsConfig *WebSearch) []responseTool {
   513â†’	if req.Config == nil || len(req.Config.Tools) == 0 {
   514â†’		return nil
   515â†’	}
   516â†’
   517â†’	var tools []responseTool
   518â†’	hasSearch := false
   519â†’
   520â†’	for _, t := range req.Config.Tools {
   521â†’		if t.GoogleSearch != nil {
   522â†’			hasSearch = true
   523â†’			continue
   524â†’		}
   525â†’		for _, fd := range t.FunctionDeclarations {
   526â†’			rt := responseTool{
   527â†’				Type:        "function",
   528â†’				Name:        fd.Name,
   529â†’				Description: fd.Description,
   530â†’			}
   531â†’			if fd.Parameters != nil {
   532â†’				rt.Parameters = schemaToMap(fd.Parameters)
   533â†’			}
   534â†’			tools = append(tools, rt)
   535â†’		}
   536â†’	}
   537â†’
   538â†’	if hasSearch {
   539â†’		wsTool := responseTool{Type: "web_search"}
   540â†’		if wsConfig != nil {
   541â†’			if wsConfig.SearchContextSize != "" {
   542â†’				wsTool.SearchContextSize = wsConfig.SearchContextSize
   543â†’			}
   544â†’			if len(wsConfig.AllowedDomains) > 0 {
   545â†’				wsTool.Filters = &searchFilters{
   546â†’					AllowedDomains: wsConfig.AllowedDomains,
   547â†’				}
   548â†’			}
   549â†’		}
   550â†’		tools = append(tools, wsTool)
   551â†’	}
   552â†’
   553â†’	return tools
   554â†’}
   555â†’
   556â†’// convertResponseOutput transforms a Responses API response into an ADK LLMResponse.
   557â†’func convertResponseOutput(resp *responsesResponse) *model.LLMResponse {
   558â†’	llmResp := &model.LLMResponse{
   559â†’		TurnComplete: true,
   560â†’	}
   561â†’
   562â†’	content := &genai.Content{Role: "model"}
   563â†’
   564â†’	for _, item := range resp.Output {
   565â†’		switch item.Type {
   566â†’		case "function_call":
   567â†’			var args map[string]any
   568â†’			if item.Arguments != "" {
   569â†’				_ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(item.Arguments), &args)
   570â†’			}
   571â†’			content.Parts = append(content.Parts, &genai.Part{
   572â†’				FunctionCall: &genai.FunctionCall{
   573â†’					ID:   item.CallID,
   574â†’					Name: item.Name,
   575â†’					Args: args,
   576â†’				},
   577â†’			})
   578â†’			llmResp.FinishReason = genai.FinishReasonStop
   579â†’
   580â†’		case "message":
   581â†’			for _, c := range item.Content {
   582â†’				if c.Text != "" {
   583â†’					content.Parts = append(content.Parts, &genai.Part{Text: c.Text})
   584â†’				}
   585â†’			}
   586â†’			llmResp.FinishReason = genai.FinishReasonStop
   587â†’
   588â†’		case "web_search_call":
   589â†’			// Informational; no ADK equivalent. Skip.
   590â†’		}
   591â†’	}
   592â†’
   593â†’	if len(content.Parts) == 0 {
   594â†’		content.Parts = append(content.Parts, &genai.Part{Text: ""})
   595â†’	}
   596â†’
   597â†’	llmResp.Content = content
   598â†’
   599â†’	if resp.Usage != nil {
   600â†’		llmResp.UsageMetadata = &genai.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata{
   601â†’			PromptTokenCount:     resp.Usage.InputTokens,
   602â†’			CandidatesTokenCount: resp.Usage.OutputTokens,
   603â†’			TotalTokenCount:      resp.Usage.TotalTokens,
   604â†’		}
   605â†’	}
   606â†’
   607â†’	return llmResp
   608â†’}

   661â†’**Step 2: Rewrite doRequest for Responses API**
   662â†’
   663â†’```go
   664â†’func (m *openaiModel) doRequest(ctx context.Context, req *model.LLMRequest) (*model.LLMResponse, error) {
   665â†’	items, instructions := convertInputs(req)
   666â†’	tools := convertResponseTools(req, m.wsConfig)
   667â†’
   668â†’	apiReq := responsesRequest{
   669â†’		Model:        m.name,
   670â†’		Input:        items,
   671â†’		Instructions: instructions,
   672â†’		Tools:        tools,
   673â†’		Store:        false,
   674â†’	}
   675â†’
   676â†’	if req.Config != nil {
   677â†’		apiReq.Temperature = req.Config.Temperature
   678â†’		apiReq.TopP = req.Config.TopP
   679â†’		if req.Config.MaxOutputTokens > 0 {
   680â†’			apiReq.MaxTokens = req.Config.MaxOutputTokens
   681â†’		}
   682â†’	}
   683â†’
   684â†’	body, err := json.Marshal(apiReq)
   685â†’	if err != nil {
   686â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: marshal request: %w", err)
   687â†’	}
   688â†’
   689â†’	httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, m.baseURL+"/responses", bytes.NewReader(body))
   690â†’	if err != nil {
   691â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: create http request: %w", err)
   692â†’	}
   693â†’	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   694â†’	httpReq.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+m.apiKey)
   695â†’
   696â†’	httpResp, err := m.client.Do(httpReq)
   697â†’	if err != nil {
   698â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: http request: %w", err)
   699â†’	}
   700â†’	defer httpResp.Body.Close()
   701â†’
   702â†’	respBody, err := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   703â†’	if err != nil {
   704â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: read response: %w", err)
   705â†’	}
   706â†’
   707â†’	if httpResp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
   708â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: API returned status %d: %s", httpResp.StatusCode, string(respBody))
   709â†’	}
   710â†’
   711â†’	var apiResp responsesResponse
   712â†’	if err := json.Unmarshal(respBody, &apiResp); err != nil {
   713â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: unmarshal response: %w", err)
   714â†’	}
   715â†’
   716â†’	if apiResp.Error != nil {
   717â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("openai: API error: %s (type=%s, code=%s)",
   718â†’			apiResp.Error.Message, apiResp.Error.Type, apiResp.Error.Code)
   719â†’	}
   720â†’
   721â†’	return convertResponseOutput(&apiResp), nil
   722â†’}
   723â†’```

   821â†’
   822â†’	// Create main model (no web search).
   823â†’	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
   824â†’	if err != nil {
   825â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
   826â†’	}
   827â†’
   828â†’	// Create project-scoped function tools.
   829â†’	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
   830â†’	if err != nil {
   831â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
   832â†’	}
   833â†’
   834â†’	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   835â†’	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   836â†’		Name:        "data_agent",
   837â†’		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   838â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   839â†’		Tools:       functionTools,
   840â†’	})
   841â†’	if err != nil {
   842â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   843â†’	}
   844â†’
   845â†’	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   846â†’	var searchTool tool.Tool
   847â†’	var searchModel model.LLM
   848â†’	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   849â†’	case "openai":
   850â†’		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   851â†’		searchTool = *wsConfig
   852â†’		// Same model, web search is a native tool in the Responses API.
   853â†’		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   854â†’		if err != nil {
   855â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   856â†’		}
   857â†’	default: // gemini
   858â†’		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   859â†’		searchModel = llmModel
   860â†’	}
   861â†’
   862â†’	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   863â†’		Name:        "search_agent",
   864â†’		Description: "Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues.",
   865â†’		Model:       searchModel,
   866â†’		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   867â†’	})
   868â†’	if err != nil {
   869â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   870â†’	}
   871â†’
   872â†’	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   873â†’	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   874â†’		Name:        "release_analyst",
   875â†’		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   876â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   877â†’		Instruction: instruction,
   878â†’		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   879â†’			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   880â†’			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   881â†’		},
   882â†’	})
   883â†’}
   884â†’```
   885â†’

# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - What you implemented
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns
```

1â†’package openai
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"fmt"
     5â†’
     6â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
     7â†’
     8â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     9â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    10â†’)
    11â†’
    12â†’// WebSearch is a tool that enables OpenAI's built-in web search via
    13â†’// web_search_options. It mirrors geminitool.GoogleSearch by adding a
    14â†’// GoogleSearch marker to the request config, which the OpenAI wrapper
    15â†’// translates into the web_search_options HTTP body field.
    16â†’type WebSearch struct{}
    17â†’
    18â†’// Name implements tool.Tool.
    19â†’func (WebSearch) Name() string { return "web_search" }
    20â†’
    21â†’// Description implements tool.Tool.
    22â†’func (WebSearch) Description() string {
    23â†’	return "Search the web for additional context about a release."
    24â†’}
    25â†’
    26â†’// IsLongRunning implements tool.Tool.
    27â†’func (WebSearch) IsLongRunning() bool { return false }
    28â†’
    29â†’// ProcessRequest implements toolinternal.RequestProcessor by adding a
    30â†’// GoogleSearch marker to the LLM request. The OpenAI model wrapper
    31â†’// detects this marker and emits web_search_options in the HTTP body.
    32â†’func (WebSearch) ProcessRequest(_ tool.Context, req *model.LLMRequest) error {
    33â†’	if req == nil {
    34â†’		return fmt.Errorf("llm request is nil")
    35â†’	}
    36â†’	if req.Config == nil {
    37â†’		req.Config = &genai.GenerateContentConfig{}
    38â†’	}
    39â†’	req.Config.Tools = append(req.Config.Tools, &genai.Tool{
    40â†’		GoogleSearch: &genai.GoogleSearch{},
    41â†’	})
    42â†’	return nil
    43â†’}
    44â†’

// --- Responses API types ---

type responsesRequest struct {
	Model        string         `json:"model"`
	Input        []inputItem    `json:"input"`
	Instructions string         `json:"instructions,omitempty"`
	Tools        []responseTool `json:"tools,omitempty"`
	Temperature  *float32       `json:"temperature,omitempty"`
	TopP         *float32       `json:"top_p,omitempty"`
	MaxTokens    int32          `json:"max_output_tokens,omitempty"`
	Store        bool           `json:"store"`
}

type inputItem struct {
	Type      string `json:"type"`               // "message", "function_call", "function_call_output"
	Role      string `json:"role,omitempty"`
	Content   string `json:"content,omitempty"`
	ID        string `json:"id,omitempty"`
	CallID    string `json:"call_id,omitempty"`
	Name      string `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Arguments string `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
	Output    string `json:"output,omitempty"`
}

type responseTool struct {
	Type              string         `json:"type"` // "function" or "web_search"
	Name              string         `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Description       string         `json:"description,omitempty"`
	Parameters        any            `json:"parameters,omitempty"`
	SearchContextSize string         `json:"search_context_size,omitempty"`
	UserLocation      any            `json:"user_location,omitempty"`
	Filters           *searchFilters `json:"filters,omitempty"`
}

type searchFilters struct {
	AllowedDomains []string `json:"allowed_domains,omitempty"`
}

type responsesResponse struct {
	ID         string       `json:"id"`
	Status     string       `json:"status"`
	Output     []outputItem `json:"output"`
	OutputText string       `json:"output_text"`
	Usage      *respUsage   `json:"usage,omitempty"`
	Error      *apiError    `json:"error,omitempty"`
}

type outputItem struct {
	Type      string          `json:"type"` // "message", "function_call", "web_search_call"
	ID        string          `json:"id,omitempty"`
	Status    string          `json:"status,omitempty"`
	CallID    string          `json:"call_id,omitempty"`
	Name      string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Arguments string          `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
	Role      string          `json:"role,omitempty"`
	Content   []outputContent `json:"content,omitempty"`
}

type outputContent struct {
	Type        string       `json:"type"` // "output_text"
	Text        string       `json:"text"`
	Annotations []annotation `json:"annotations,omitempty"`
}

type annotation struct {
	Type       string `json:"type"` // "url_citation"
	URL        string `json:"url"`
	Title      string `json:"title"`
	StartIndex int    `json:"start_index"`
	EndIndex   int    `json:"end_index"`
}

type respUsage struct {
	InputTokens  int32 `json:"input_tokens"`
	OutputTokens int32 `json:"output_tokens"`
	TotalTokens  int32 `json:"total_tokens"`
}

type apiError struct {
	Message string `json:"message"`
	Type    string `json:"type"`
	Code    string `json:"code"`
}

API equivalents. Add SearchContextSize and AllowedDomains config fields
to WebSearch. Update NewModel to accept optional *WebSearch parameter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
All four tasks are complete. Here is a summary of everything that was changed and the test results.

---

## Changes Made

### Task 1: WebSearch config fields

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`**
- Added `SearchContextSize string` and `AllowedDomains []string` fields to the `WebSearch` struct
- Updated the doc comment to reference "Responses API" instead of "web_search_options"

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go`**
- Renamed `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest` to `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_SetsMarker`
- Test now constructs `WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "high"}` and verifies the field persists alongside the GoogleSearch marker

### Tasks 2-3: Replace types and conversion functions

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/openai.go`**

Removed old Chat Completions types:
- `webSearchOptions`, `chatRequest`, `chatMessage`, `chatTool`, `chatFunction`, `chatResponse`, `chatChoice`, `chatUsage`, `toolCall`, `functionCall`

Added new Responses API types:
- `responsesRequest`, `inputItem`, `responseTool`, `searchFilters`, `responsesResponse`, `outputItem`, `outputContent`, `annotation`, `respUsage`, `apiError`

Removed old conversion functions:
- `convertContents`, `convertTools`, `convertResponse`, `hasWebSearch`, `mergeAssistantToolCalls`

Added new conversion functions:
- `convertInputs(req) ([]inputItem, string)` -- converts ADK Contents to input items; extracts system instruction as separate `instructions` string
- `convertResponseTools(req, wsConfig) []responseTool` -- converts ADK tools to flat Responses API format; maps GoogleSearch marker to `{type: "web_search"}` with config from wsConfig
- `convertResponseOutput(resp) *model.LLMResponse` -- converts Responses API output items to ADK LLMResponse; handles `function_call`, `message`, and skips `web_search_call` items

Kept unchanged: `schemaToMap`, `mapRole`, `mapFinishReason`

### Task 4: Update model struct and doRequest

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/openai.go`**
- Added `wsConfig *WebSearch` field to `openaiModel` struct
- Updated `NewModel` signature to `NewModel(_ context.Context, modelName string, cfg Config, wsConfig *WebSearch) (model.LLM, error)`
- Rewrote `doRequest` to build `responsesRequest`, POST to `/responses` endpoint, parse `responsesResponse`, and convert via `convertResponseOutput`

### Tests

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go`**

Removed:
- `TestConvertTools_SkipsGoogleSearchMarker`
- `TestHasWebSearch`

Added:
- `TestConvertInputs_SystemInstruction` -- verifies system instruction extraction
- `TestConvertInputs_FunctionCallAndResponse` -- verifies function_call and function_call_output input item conversion
- `TestConvertResponseTools_WebSearch` -- verifies function tools and web_search tool with config
- `TestConvertResponseOutput_TextAndFunctionCalls` -- verifies output conversion of function calls and text messages

Kept: `TestSchemaToMap_LowercasesType`

## Test Results

All 9 tests pass:

```
=== RUN   TestSchemaToMap_LowercasesType              --- PASS
=== RUN   TestConvertInputs_SystemInstruction          --- PASS
=== RUN   TestConvertInputs_FunctionCallAndResponse    --- PASS
=== RUN   TestConvertResponseTools_WebSearch            --- PASS
=== RUN   TestConvertResponseOutput_TextAndFunctionCalls --- PASS
=== RUN   TestWebSearch_Name                           --- PASS
=== RUN   TestWebSearch_IsLongRunning                  --- PASS
=== RUN   TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_SetsMarker      --- PASS
=== RUN   TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_NilRequest      --- PASS
PASS
ok  github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai  0.709s
```

**Note:** The `NewModel` signature change (4th parameter `wsConfig *WebSearch`) will temporarily break the caller in `internal/agent/model.go` -- that gets fixed in the next task (Tasks 5-6).

# Spec Compliance Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a spec compliance reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementer built what was requested (nothing more, nothing less)

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Review spec compliance for Task N"
  prompt: |
    You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

    ## What Was Requested

    [FULL TEXT of task requirements]

    ## What Implementer Claims They Built

    [From implementer's report]

    ## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

    The implementer finished suspiciously quickly. Their report may be incomplete,
    inaccurate, or optimistic. You MUST verify everything independently.

    **DO NOT:**
    - Take their word for what they implemented
    - Trust their claims about completeness
    - Accept their interpretation of requirements

    **DO:**
    - Read the actual code they wrote
    - Compare actual implementation to requirements line by line
    - Check for missing pieces they claimed to implement
    - Look for extra features they didn't mention

    ## Your Job

    Read the implementation code and verify:

    **Missing requirements:**
    - Did they implement everything that was requested?
    - Are there requirements they skipped or missed?
    - Did they claim something works but didn't actually implement it?

    **Extra/unneeded work:**
    - Did they build things that weren't requested?
    - Did they over-engineer or add unnecessary features?
    - Did they add "nice to haves" that weren't in spec?

    **Misunderstandings:**
    - Did they interpret requirements differently than intended?
    - Did they solve the wrong problem?
    - Did they implement the right feature but wrong way?

    **Verify by reading code, not by trusting report.**

    Report:
    - âś… Spec compliant (if everything matches after code inspection)
    - âťŚ Issues found: [list specifically what's missing or extra, with file:line references]
```

input.prompt:
You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

## What Was Requested

Tasks 1-4 of the OpenAI Responses API migration:

1. **WebSearch tool**: Add `SearchContextSize string` and `AllowedDomains []string` fields to `WebSearch` struct. Update doc comment to reference "Responses API". Rename test to `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_SetsMarker`.

2. **Replace types**: Remove old Chat Completions types (`webSearchOptions`, `chatRequest`, `chatMessage`, `chatTool`, `chatFunction`, `chatResponse`, `chatChoice`, `chatUsage`, `toolCall`, `functionCall`). Add new Responses API types (`responsesRequest`, `inputItem`, `responseTool`, `searchFilters`, `responsesResponse`, `outputItem`, `outputContent`, `annotation`, `respUsage`, `apiError`).

3. **New conversion functions**: Replace `convertContents`, `convertTools`, `convertResponse`, `hasWebSearch`, `mergeAssistantToolCalls` with:
   - `convertInputs(req) ([]inputItem, string)` â€” extracts system instruction, maps FunctionCallâ†’function_call, FunctionResponseâ†’function_call_output, textâ†’message
   - `convertResponseTools(req, wsConfig) []responseTool` â€” flat function tools (NOT nested `function` key), GoogleSearch markerâ†’web_search tool with config
   - `convertResponseOutput(resp) *model.LLMResponse` â€” function_callâ†’FunctionCall (use `call_id` as ID), messageâ†’text, skip web_search_call

4. **Model struct & doRequest**: Add `wsConfig *WebSearch` to `openaiModel`. Update `NewModel` to accept `wsConfig *WebSearch` as 4th param. Rewrite `doRequest` to POST to `/responses`, build `responsesRequest` with `Store: false`, parse `responsesResponse`.

5. **Tests**: Remove `TestConvertTools_SkipsGoogleSearchMarker` and `TestHasWebSearch`. Add `TestConvertInputs_SystemInstruction`, `TestConvertInputs_FunctionCallAndResponse`, `TestConvertResponseTools_WebSearch`, `TestConvertResponseOutput_TextAndFunctionCalls`. Keep `TestSchemaToMap_LowercasesType`.

## What Implementer Claims They Built

All of the above. All 9 tests pass.

## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

Read the actual code and verify everything line by line.

## Your Job

Read these files and verify against the spec:
- `internal/agent/openai/openai.go`
- `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go`
- `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`
- `internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go`

Verify:
- All old types and functions are gone
- All new types match the spec (field names, json tags, types)
- New functions have correct behavior
- `doRequest` POSTs to `/responses` not `/chat/completions`
- `NewModel` accepts 4th param `wsConfig *WebSearch`
- Tests match spec

Report âś… or âťŚ with specific file:line references.
