content.text:
Found it. Line 75: `agentpkg.BuildAgent(ctx, store, project, llmConfig, "")` â€” the version is hardcoded as empty string `""`. Then in `BuildAgent`, `strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)` replaces all `{{VERSION}}` placeholders with empty string, producing instructions like "analyzing version  of a software project" and "Focus ONLY on version ." â€” which confuses the LLM.

This isn't a regression from our changes â€” this was pre-existing. But let me check if there's a version flag that should exist.

content.content: No matches found

content.text:
There's no `--version` flag on the agent CLI. The dev CLI passes `""` as the version to `BuildAgent`, which causes the `{{VERSION}}` placeholders to become empty strings in the agent instruction. The LLM then sees "analyzing version  of a software project" and gets confused.

The fix is straightforward â€” add a `--version` flag to the agent CLI.

input.old_string:
projectID := flag.String("project-id", "", "Project ID to scope the agent to (required)")
	flag.Parse()
input.new_string:
projectID := flag.String("project-id", "", "Project ID to scope the agent to (required)")
	version := flag.String("version", "", "Target version to analyze (e.g. v1.10.15)")
	flag.Parse()

const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.

Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.

Use the available tools to:
1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).

CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting â€” just the raw JSON.

For download_links: prefer direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) over generic release pages. Include the general release page URL as well, but prioritize direct download links when available.

The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
{
  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
}`

content.text:
The instruction is version-centric â€” every sentence references `{{VERSION}}`. When version is empty, it becomes nonsensical. The production path (`executeAgent`) also has this issue but handles it differently â€” it falls back to "Analyze the recent releases" as the user prompt (line 325), but the system instruction still has empty placeholders.

The cleanest fix: when version is empty, use an alternative instruction that doesn't reference a specific version.
The fix should be in `BuildAgent` â€” when version is empty, use a generic instruction instead of one riddled with empty `{{VERSION}}` placeholders.

1â†’package main
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"flag"
     6â†’	"fmt"
     7â†’	"log"
     8â†’	"log/slog"
     9â†’	"os"
    10â†’
    11â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    12â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/cmd/launcher"
    13â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/cmd/launcher/full"
    14â†’
    15â†’	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    16â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    17â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    18â†’)
    19â†’
    20â†’func main() {
    21â†’	projectID := flag.String("project-id", "", "Project ID to scope the agent to (required)")
    22â†’	version := flag.String("version", "", "Target version to analyze (e.g. v1.10.15)")
    23â†’	flag.Parse()
    24â†’
    25â†’	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    26â†’	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    27â†’	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    28â†’	case "debug":
    29â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    30â†’	case "warn":
    31â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    32â†’	case "error":
    33â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    34â†’	}
    35â†’	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    36â†’
    37â†’	if *projectID == "" {
    38â†’		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error: --project-id is required")
    39â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "usage: go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> [--version=<version>] [web api webui]\n")
    40â†’		os.Exit(1)
    41â†’	}
    42â†’
    43â†’	ctx := context.Background()
    44â†’
    45â†’	// Database
    46â†’	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    47â†’	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    48â†’	if err != nil {
    49â†’		log.Fatalf("database connection failed: %v", err)
    50â†’	}
    51â†’	defer pool.Close()
    52â†’
    53â†’	store := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    54â†’
    55â†’	// Validate project exists
    56â†’	project, err := store.GetProject(ctx, *projectID)
    57â†’	if err != nil {
    58â†’		log.Fatalf("project %q not found: %v", *projectID, err)
    59â†’	}
    60â†’	slog.Info("loaded project", "id", project.ID, "name", project.Name)
    61â†’
    62â†’	// LLM config (same env vars as server)
    63â†’	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    64â†’	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    65â†’	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    66â†’		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    67â†’	}
    68â†’	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    69â†’		Provider:      llmProvider,
    70â†’		Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    71â†’		GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    72â†’		OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    73â†’		OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    74â†’	}
    75â†’
    76â†’	agentInstance, err := agentpkg.BuildAgent(ctx, store, project, llmConfig, *version)
    77â†’	if err != nil {
    78â†’		log.Fatalf("build agent: %v", err)
    79â†’	}
    80â†’
    81â†’	// Launch with ADK-Web UI
    82â†’	config := &launcher.Config{
    83â†’		AgentLoader: agent.NewSingleLoader(agentInstance),
    84â†’	}
    85â†’
    86â†’	l := full.NewLauncher()
    87â†’	if err := l.Execute(ctx, config, flag.Args()); err != nil {
    88â†’		log.Fatalf("launcher failed: %v\n\n%s", err, l.CommandLineSyntax())
    89â†’	}
    90â†’}
    91â†’
    92â†’func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
    93â†’	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
    94â†’		return v
    95â†’	}
    96â†’	return fallback
    97â†’}
    98â†’

content.text:
Fixed. Two changes:

1. **`cmd/agent/main.go`** â€” Added `--version` flag so you can now run:
   ```
   go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> --version=v1.10.15 web
   ```

2. **`internal/agent/orchestrator.go`** â€” When version is empty, `{{VERSION}}` placeholders are replaced with sensible generic text ("the latest releases of" / "the latest version") instead of blank strings. This prevents the LLM from seeing broken instructions like "analyzing version  of a software project".
